articles.title: Riots, violence, hate: Anti-immigrant unrest spells danger in Belfast
articles.summary: Crowds exploiting a knife attack are inflicting terror on Northern Ireland's ethnic minority communities.
articles.titleZh: 骚乱、暴力、仇恨：反移民骚乱给贝尔法斯特带来危险
articles.summaryZh: 人群利用持刀袭击事件正在给北爱尔兰的少数民族社区造成恐怖。

contentParagraphs.en: Belfast, Northern Ireland – When violence broke out in east Belfast near Zeinab’s home, as a mother of three from Sudan, she felt terrified.Anti-immigrant rioters have carried out a wave of racist attacks in the Northern Irish capital after a knife attack on Wednesday.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4UK PM Starmer slams ‘unjustified’ anti-immigration violence in Belfastlist 2 of 4Police in Belfast use water cannon as anti-immigrant unrest continueslist 3 of 4Anti-immigrant protesters clash with police, torch car at Belfast rallylist 4 of 4Photos: Belfast police fire water cannon at anti-immigration protestsend of listThe alleged assailant, a 30-year-old Sudanese national who entered Northern Ireland through Ireland, has been charged with attempted murder. The victim, 44-year-old Stephen Ogilvie, remains in hospital with life-changing injuries to his face and back, having reportedly lost an eye.“We strongly condemn and reject what happened,” said Zeinab, who requested to withhold her surname. “Unfortunately, it turned out that (the suspect) is of Sudanese nationality. But this is the opposite of what is known about our Sudanese people. They are kind people, known for their generosity, their morals, and the way they treat others.”As agitators burned homes and businesses they believed to be owned by ethnic minorities, Zeinab asked an NGO, the Anaka Women’s Collective, to evacuate her family.Like other people of colour, she was welcomed by an Irish family and is now taking shelter outside Belfast.“May God reward them with all goodness. We cannot describe what they have done for us,” she said. “We feel that not everyone here is unaccepting of foreigners. There is goodness, there are people who love us, people who shared their homes with us, shared our worries, shared our moments of weakness, and took us in.”A woman carries food containers to deliver to vulnerable migrants, following a knife attack on June 8 that left a man seriously injured and prompted police to declare it a critical incident, in Belfast, Northern Ireland, June 11, 2026 [Isabel Infantes/Reuters]On Tuesday evening, an eerie silence took hold of the city as panicked local traders hurriedly pulled up their shutters, locking up early after threats issued on social media earlier that day.A list created by AI, shared by prominent figures such as Tommy Robinson and Elon Musk, warned “All Bunnesses” shut up shop by 5.30pm. It included names of streets in the Northern Irish capital. A second list showed some 70 locations in the United Kingdom, also shared about the same time.“The whole of the United Kingdom is hitting the streets tonight at 7pm following yet another invader attack on our people,” far-right agitator Robinson said.Hundreds heeded the call.Young boys, some of whom looked no older than 13, marched determinedly in the direction of east Belfast’s Lower Newtownards Road.Within an hour, several loud bangs were heard as a bus and other vehicles were set alight, sending plumes of smoke into the drizzly June evening. Some perpetrators were understood to have been as young as 10.On the street, the words “F*** Islam” were graffitied on the shutters of a halal butcher shop.Anti-Islam sentiment appears to be a “more prominent feature” in these riots, Amnesty International’s Northern Ireland Director Patrick Corrigan told Al Jazeera, compared with other episodes of unrest.Several cars and homes across Belfast were set ablaze in the violence [Tommy Greene/Al Jazeera]As video footage showed, crowds would go on to smash windows, kick down doors and attempt to intimidate or burn out residents from nearby houses where they believed migrants were living. A large group of adults watched as the young rioters kicked up mayhem, terrorising Belfast’s relatively small but growing ethnic minority communities and claiming the night – and this part of town – as theirs.Only 3.4 percent of Northern Ireland’s population, just over 65,000 people, were found to be from an ethnic minority background in the 2022 census. In a country of 1.9 million, just 2,379 are seeking asylum. But as Corrigan has noted, this is the “third consecutive summer in which Northern Ireland has experienced organised racist violence, with each outbreak more serious than the last”.The scenes echo coordinated attacks in England over recent years, as a familiar pattern plays out: Riots in the wake of a crime, alleged or proven, if the accused or guilty party is not white.As in the recent case of the killing of Henry Nowak, a teenager murdered in southern England, the victim’s family in Belfast has said the street disorder is “not welcome”, condemning attempts to “divide people or fuel hostility”.‘No HMOs’ was graffitied on a building. Houses in Multiple Occupation are often an affordable entry point for new migrants as a shared housing setup [Tommy Greene]Belfast closed down ‘because of fear’For Northern Ireland’s victims of crime commissioner, Geraldine Hanna, the ability of distant and faceless digital actors to rapidly cripple the region’s largest city represents a power she believes the devolved government at Stormont does not have.“Basically, on the back of a GIF on social media, Belfast closes down,” she said. “Schools close, public transport closes, businesses close. Because of the fear.”Hanna said one protest planned for Wednesday did not go ahead in the end.“But [nevertheless] some faceless online actor was able to close down Belfast.”She told Al Jazeera the attacks should be treated as “domestic terrorism” and questioned why the government was not addressing them as such, leaving civil society actors such as charities to plug gaps in immediate response, mutual aid and formulating counter-strategies.Other demonstrations in larger urban centres across Northern Ireland were joined by crowds dressed in black and covering their faces. Some masked men set up informal checkpoints, asking drivers passing by if any passengers were the “foreigners” they boasted about
contentParagraphs.zh: 北爱尔兰贝尔法斯特 - 当贝尔法斯特东部在泽纳布家附近爆发暴力事件时，作为来自苏丹的三个孩子的母亲，她感到害怕。周三发生持刀袭击事件后，反移民暴徒在北爱尔兰首都发起了一波种族主义袭击。推荐故事列表 4 项列表 1 of 4 英国首相斯塔默猛烈抨击贝尔法斯特“不合理”的反移民暴力列表 2 of 4 贝尔法斯特警察使用反移民骚乱仍在继续使用水枪list 3 of 4反移民抗议者与警察发生冲突，在贝尔法斯特集会上火炬车list 4 of 4照片：贝尔法斯特警察在反移民抗议中发射水枪发送列表被指控的袭击者是一名30岁的苏丹国民，通过爱尔兰进入北爱尔兰，被指控谋杀未遂。受害者是 44 岁的史蒂芬·奥格尔维 (Stephen Ogilvie)，目前仍在医院接受治疗，面部和背部受伤，据报道失去了一只眼睛。“我们强烈谴责并拒绝所发生的事情，”要求隐去姓氏的泽纳布说。 “不幸的是，事实证明（嫌疑人）是苏丹籍。但这与我们对苏丹人民的了解相反。他们是善良的人，以慷慨、道德和对待他人的方式而闻名。”当煽动者烧毁他们认为属于少数族裔拥有的房屋和企业时，泽纳布要求非政府组织阿纳卡妇女集体疏散她的家人。像其他有色人种一样，她受到爱尔兰人的欢迎家人现在正在贝尔法斯特郊外避难。“愿上帝用一切善意回报他们。我们无法描述他们为我们所做的一切，”她说。 “我们觉得并不是这里的每个人都不接受外国人。这里有善良的人，有人爱我们，有人与我们分享家园，分担我们的忧虑，分担我们的软弱时刻，并收留了我们。” 2026 年 6 月 11 日，北爱尔兰贝尔法斯特，一名妇女搬运食品容器，准备运送给弱势移民。6 月 8 日，北爱尔兰贝尔法斯特发生持刀袭击事件，导致一名男子严重受伤，警方宣布这是一起严重事件 [Isabel Infantes/路透社]周二晚上，整个城市陷入了诡异的寂静，当天早些时候社交媒体上发出威胁后，惊慌失措的当地商人匆忙拉上百叶窗，提前关门。汤米·罗宾逊 (Tommy Robinson) 和埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 等知名人士分享的人工智能创建的名单警告“所有 Bunnesses”在下午 5 点 30 分之前关门歇业。它包括北爱尔兰首都的街道名称。第二份名单显示了英国约 70 个地点，也大约在同一时间分享。极右翼煽动者罗宾逊说：“今晚 7 点，整个英国都走上街头，再次遭到入侵者对我们人民的袭击。”公共汽车和其他车辆被点燃，在细雨蒙蒙的六月傍晚冒出滚滚浓烟。据了解，一些肇事者年仅 10 岁。在街上，一家清真肉店的百叶窗上涂鸦着“F*** Islam”的字样。大赦国际北爱尔兰主任帕特里克·科里根 (Patrick Corrigan) 告诉半岛电视台，与其他骚乱事件相比，反伊斯兰情绪似乎是这些骚乱中的“更突出的特征”。贝尔法斯特的几辆汽车和房屋在暴力事件中被纵火焚烧。格林/半岛电视台]正如视频片段所示，人群会继续砸碎窗户、踢倒门，并试图恐吓或烧死他们认为是移民居住的附近房屋的居民。一大群成年人目睹了年轻的骚乱者制造混乱，恐吓贝尔法斯特相对较小但不断增长的少数民族社区，并声称这个夜晚和城镇的这一部分是他们的。在 2022 年人口普查中，只有 3.4% 的北爱尔兰人口（刚刚超过 65,000 人）来自少数民族背景。在这个拥有 190 万人口的国家中，只有 2,379 人正在寻求庇护。但正如科里根所指出的，这是“北爱尔兰连续第三个夏天经历有组织的种族主义暴力，每次爆发都比上一次更严重”。这些场景与近年来英格兰发生的协调一致的袭击相呼应，正如一个熟悉的模式上演的那样：如果被告或有罪方不是白人，指控或证实的犯罪后就会发生骚乱。就像最近在英格兰南部被谋杀的青少年亨利·诺瓦克被杀的案件一样，贝尔法斯特的受害者家人表示，街头骚乱“并不严重”。欢迎”，谴责“分裂人民或助长敌意”的企图。建筑物上被涂鸦“没有 HMO”。对于新移民来说，多住所往往是一个负担得起的切入点，因为贝尔法斯特的共享住房设施“因为恐惧”而关闭。对于北爱尔兰犯罪专员杰拉尔丁·汉纳（Geraldine Hanna）的受害者来说，遥远且不露面的数字行为者能够迅速削弱该地区最大的城市，这代表了她认为斯托蒙特的权力下放政府所不具备的力量。“基本上，在社交媒体上的 GIF 的背后，贝尔法斯特关闭了，”她说。 “学校关闭，公共交通关闭，企业关闭。因为恐惧。”汉纳说，计划于周三举行的一场抗议活动最终没有进行。“但（尽管如此）一些不露面的网络参与者能够关闭贝尔法斯特。”她告诉半岛电视台，这些袭击应该被视为“国内恐怖主义”，并质疑政府为什么不这样处理它们，让慈善机构等民间社会行动者通过立即反应、互助和制定方案来填补空白。反策略。在北爱尔兰较大的城市中心举行的其他示威活动中，也有身穿黑衣、遮住脸的人群参加。一些蒙面男子设置非正式检查站，询问路过的司机是否有乘客是他们吹嘘的“外国人”

articles.title: Who is Elon Musk and what is his net worth?
articles.summary: The boss of X, Tesla and SpaceX is the world's richest person - and might even be about to become a trillionaire.
articles.titleZh: 谁是埃隆·马斯克？他的净资产是多少？
articles.summaryZh: X、特斯拉和 SpaceX 的老板是世界首富，甚至可能即将成为万亿富翁。

contentParagraphs.en: Site search1 hour agoLiv McMahon, Natalie Sherman, Dearbail Jordan & Osmond ChiaBusiness & technology reportersGetty ImagesThe Tesla boss became the first person ever to achieve a net worth of more than $500bnIt seems like not a day goes by without billionaire Elon Musk making headlines - and soon, we may even be calling him a trillionaire.The boss of SpaceX, Tesla and X (formerly Twitter) is the world's richest person.But Musk is known for more than simply his immense wealth.The billionaire has used his social media platform to air his views on a vast array of topics, ranging from current affairs to the future of humanity.In early 2024, his reach expanded further after Donald Trump's victory in the US presidential election - in which the Tesla executive played a key but controversial role - before their relationship imploded in a bitter feud.Musk has not only waded into political controversy in the US, but also across the Atlantic - where his posts and comments about matters in the UK, Germany and other European states have frequently angered politicians.These forays into politics have, however, sometimes appeared to come at a cost to his businesses. Analysts attributed slumps in Tesla sales in 2025 to be partly due to customers turning against Musk.Where was Elon Musk born?Born in Pretoria, South Africa, Musk showed his talents for entrepreneurship early, going door-to-door with his brother selling homemade chocolate Easter eggs and developing his first computer game at the age of 12.He has described his childhood as difficult, affected by his parents' divorce, bullying at school and his own difficulty picking up on social cues because of Asperger's Syndrome. At the earliest opportunity, he left home for college, moving to Canada and then the US, where he studied economics and physics at the University of Pennsylvania, an Ivy League college.In a 2010 essay for Marie Claire, his first wife, Justine Musk, a writer whom he met in college and married in 2000, wrote that even before making his millions Musk was "not a man who takes no for an answer"."The will to compete and dominate, that made him so successful in business, did not magically shut off when he came home," she recalled, adding that he told her while dancing at their wedding, "I am the alpha in this relationship."How did Elon Musk make his money?After being accepted to a physics graduate degree programme at Stanford University, Musk quickly dropped out and founded two technology start-ups during the "dotcom boom" of the 1990s. These included a web software firm and an online banking company that eventually became PayPal, which was sold to eBay in 2002 for $1.5bn (£1.2bn).He ploughed his fortune into a new rocket company, SpaceX - which he aimed to make a cost-effective alternative to Nasa - and a new electric car company, Tesla, where he chaired the board until becoming chief executive in 2008.Getty ImagesElon Musk in 2004The two firms have been credited with upending their industries, even as they sometimes veered close to financial collapse.Other business ventures include his takeover of social media platform Twitter in October 2022.Musk's long-term ambition is for X to become an "everything app" offering a range of services. However, the value of the firm plunged from the $44bn he originally paid to just $9.4bn, according to some estimates.Leaving X can be a tricky decision for brandsHe also has ambitions in the AI sector, being an early investor in ChatGPT's parent company before parting ways in 2018, and setting up his own company xAI "to understand the true nature of the universe" in 2023."I'm never hugely convinced that he knows what he wants to do tomorrow," says journalist Chris Stokel-Walker of Musk's wide-ranging interests. "He very much leads by instinct."In a 2015 biography, author Ashlee Vance described Musk as "a confrontational know-it-all" with an "abundant ego". But he also called him an awkward dancer and diffident public speaker.In the press, he's been dubbed both a mad genius and X's biggest troll - known as much for his lofty ambitions as his petty fights, not to mention the more serious lawsuits he and his companies have faced from regulators, investors and others over issues such as racial discrimination and the trustworthiness of his claims.Divorced three times - twice from the same woman, British actress Talulah Riley - Musk is frank about his faults."But if you put those against the things I've done right, it makes much more sense."What is Elon Musk's net worth?Those contradictions certainly haven't stopped Musk from amassing a fortune.In October 2025, he became the first person to achieve a net worth of more than half a trillion dollars, according to Forbes, which tracks the wealth of billionaires.Musk already sits well above wealthy tech billionaires topping rich lists, including Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin, Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, Amazon founder Jeff Bezos and Facebook creator Mark Zuckerberg.But when SpaceX becomes a publicly traded company on 12 June, he is poised to become even more wealthy.The publication says if SpaceX's shares float at the planned offer price of $135 a share, his net worth would rise to about $988bn.Among a list of goals, he would need to grow Tesla's value eightfold, sell a million AI robots and another 12 million Tesla cars.Throughout 2024 Musk was locked in a legal battle over a $56bn pay package from Tesla. In December 2024, a Delaware judge rejected his claim to it for a second time, but in December 2025 the package was reinstated by the Delaware Supreme Court.What does Elon Musk do with all his money?Why human-shaped robots loom large in Musk's Tesla plansGetty ImagesElon Musk and his son X Æ A-12 in 2021. Musk, who has fathered 14 children, has called "population collapse" a bigger risk than global warmingMusk also champions digital currencies and has a hand in several other smaller companies, including tunnel-maker the Boring Company.Musk, w
contentParagraphs.zh: 网站搜索 1 小时前丽芙·麦克马洪 (Liv McMahon)、娜塔莉·谢尔曼 (Natalie Sherman)、迪尔拜尔·乔丹 (Dearbail Jordan) 和奥斯蒙德·谢 (Osmond Chia) 商业与科技记者盖蒂图片社特斯拉老板成为有史以来第一个净资产超过 5000 亿美元的人似乎每一天亿万富翁埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 都会成为头条新闻 - 很快，我们甚至可能称他为万亿富翁。 SpaceX、特斯拉和 X（前身为 Twitter）的老板是世界上最富有的人。但马斯克不仅仅因其巨大的财富而闻名。这位亿万富翁利用他的社交媒体平台就从时事到人类未来的广泛话题表达了自己的观点。2024 年初，唐纳德·特朗普在美国总统大选中获胜后，他的影响力进一步扩大，特斯拉高管在其中发挥了关键但有争议的作用，随后他们的关系陷入了激烈的争斗。马斯克不仅卷入了美国的政治争议，还涉足了大西洋彼岸，他在英国、德国和其他欧洲国家的帖子和评论引起了人们的关注。经常激怒政客。然而，这些涉足政治有时似乎是以牺牲他的企业为代价的。分析师将 2025 年特斯拉销量下滑的部分原因归咎于客户反对马斯克。埃隆·马斯克出生在哪里？出生于南非比勒陀利亚，马斯克很早就展现了自己的创业才能，与他的兄弟挨家挨户推销自制的巧克力复活节彩蛋，并在 12 岁时开发了他的第一个电脑游戏。他形容自己的童年很艰难，受到父母离婚、学校欺凌以及自己因阿斯伯格综合症而难以接受社交暗示的影响。综合症。他一有机会就离家上大学，搬到了加拿大，然后去了美国，在常春藤盟校宾夕法尼亚大学学习经济学和物理学。他的第一任妻子、作家贾斯汀·马斯克（他在大学里认识并于 2000 年结婚）在 2010 年为《嘉人》杂志撰写的一篇文章中写道，即使在赚到数百万美元之前，马斯克也“不是一个不接受拒绝的人”。 “当他回家时，他就关机了。”她回忆道，并补充说，他在婚礼上跳舞时告诉她，“我是这段关系中的阿尔法。”埃隆·马斯克是如何赚钱的？在被斯坦福大学物理研究生学位课程录取后，马斯克很快就退学了，并在 20 世纪 90 年代的“互联网繁荣”期间创办了两家科技初创公司。其中包括一家网络软件公司和一家在线银行公司，最终成为 PayPal，该公司于 2002 年以 15 亿美元（12 亿英镑）的价格出售给 eBay。他将自己的财富投入了一家新的火箭公司 SpaceX（他的目标是成为 NASA 的一个具有成本效益的替代品）和一家新的电动汽车公司特斯拉（Tesla），他在该公司担任董事会主席，直到 2008 年成为首席执行官。盖蒂图片社埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）于 2004 年被认为是这两家公司的创始人。颠覆他们的行业，即使他们有时接近财务崩溃。其他商业冒险包括他在 2022 年 10 月收购社交媒体平台 Twitter。马斯克的长期目标是让 X 成为提供一系列服务的“一切应用程序”。然而，根据一些估计，该公司的价值从他最初支付的 440 亿美元骤降到只有 94 亿美元。对于品牌来说，离开 X 可能是一个棘手的决定。他在人工智能领域也有雄心，在 2018 年分道扬镳之前是 ChatGPT 母公司的早期投资者，并在 2023 年成立了自己的公司 xAI，“以了解宇宙的真实本质”。“我从来没有非常相信他知道自己想做什么明天，”记者克里斯·斯托克尔-沃克谈到马斯克广泛的兴趣时说道。 “他非常靠本能来领导。” 在 2015 年的一本传记中，作者阿什莉·万斯 (Ashlee Vance) 将马斯克描述为“一个具有对抗性的万事通”，具有“丰富的自我”。但他也称他是一个笨拙的舞者和缺乏自信的公众演讲者。在媒体上，他被称为疯狂的天才和 X 最大的巨魔 - 他因远大的野心和小打小闹而闻名，更不用说他和他的公司因种族歧视和他的主张的可信度等问题而面临来自监管机构、投资者和其他人的更严重的诉讼。 英国女演员塔露拉·莱利 (Talulah Riley) 离婚过 3 次，两次与同一个女人离婚 - 马斯克对自己的婚姻很坦诚。 “但如果你把这些与我做对的事情相比，那就更有意义了。”埃隆·马斯克的净资产是多少？这些矛盾当然没有阻止马斯克积累财富。根据追踪亿万富翁财富的《福布斯》，2025 年 10 月，他成为第一个净资产超过 5000 亿美元的人。马斯克已经远远高于富豪榜上的科技亿万富翁，其中包括谷歌联合创始人拉里佩奇和谢尔盖·布林、甲骨文联合创始人拉里·埃里森、亚马逊创始人杰夫·贝佐斯和 Facebook 创始人马克·扎克伯格。但当 SpaceX 于 6 月 12 日成为上市公司时，他准备变得更加富有。该出版物称，如果 SpaceX 的股票按照每股 135 美元的计划发行价浮动，他的净资产将升至约 9880 亿美元。在一系列目标中，他需要将特斯拉的价值增长八倍，出售 100 万台人工智能机器人以及另外 1200 万辆特斯拉汽车。 2024 年，马斯克因特斯拉 560 亿美元的薪酬方案陷入法律纠纷。 2024 年 12 月，特拉华州法官第二次驳回了他的主张，但在 2025 年 12 月，特拉华州最高法院恢复了该包裹。埃隆·马斯克用他所有的钱做什么？为什么人形机器人在马斯克的特斯拉计划中占据重要地位盖蒂图片埃隆·马斯克和他的儿子 X Æ A-12 在 2021 年。马斯克已经是 14 个孩子的父亲，他称“人口崩溃”是比全球人口更大的风险马斯克还倡导数字货币，并参与了其他几家较小的公司，包括隧道制造商 Boring Company。

articles.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX raises $75bn ahead of world's biggest stock market launch
articles.summary: The public sale is also expected to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.
articles.titleZh: 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的 SpaceX 在全球最大规模的股市上市前筹集了 750 亿美元资金
articles.summaryZh: 此次公开出售也有望使埃隆·马斯克成为世界上第一位万亿富翁。

contentParagraphs.en: Elon Musk's SpaceX raises $75bn ahead of world's biggest stock market launch29 minutes agoKali Hays,Technology reporterandJemma Crew,Business reporterGetty ImagesSpaceX has raised $75bn (£56bn) from financial firms ahead of it becoming a publicly traded company on Friday, in what is expected to be the highest-value stock listing in history.In a filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission, the space exploration and artificial intelligence (AI) company said it had sold $75bn in shares priced at $135 each.The share price matches the estimate SpaceX gave last week, leaving the firm's expected initial stock market value to be nearly $1.8tn.At that value, chief executive Elon Musk - already the richest man in the world - is set to become the world's first trillionaire.SpaceX is selling only 5% of the total shares available in the company at first.Once these shares start trading, their value could rise or fall depending on how many shares are made available for sale in the Initial Public Offering (IPO), and how strong the demand is for those shares.If the company's shares sell at or above $135 when trading opens on Friday, SpaceX will immediately become one of the most valuable public companies in the world. However, it is up to investors to decide if they think the shares are worth that much.Interest in acquiring a stake in SpaceX among investment funds and individuals, often referred to as "retail investors," is increasingly expected to be high.Certain financial analysts have already set target prices for the shares above SpaceX's $135 estimate, including the global brokerage Oppenheimer which said on Thursday it expects the company to hit $190 a share.The public price for a share in the company is ultimately decided through what is essentially an auction on the open stock market.Peta CooperPeta Cooper is excited about investing in SpaceXPeta Cooper, 43, a copywriter who lives in Cornwall, is one of those planning to buy SpaceX shares.Cooper, who grew up in California and who already has a portfolio of shares mainly in tech and crypto firms, plans to invest about £750."It's really exciting. I really love the space industry. [SpaceX] have had a really great track record so far with their launches and their innovation," she said.While she expects the share price to dip at some point, she plans to hold onto the stock in the long term."I plan to keep it in my portfolio and let it grow."Many are seeing today's SpaceX IPO as a huge bet on AI, and some analysts are sceptical."It's a huge roll of the dice", says Sinead O'Sullivan, an economist who has worked at Nasa"There's so much built into one company, one share price here. Do I believe that Elon Musk is great at executing technological innovation? Yes. Do I believe that the share price is representative of the future value he's going to create? Probably not..."Tom Mueller, the first official employee of SpaceX and now the founder of Impulse Space, told the BBC's Michelle Fleury "it's unbelievable" to see what the company has become.Mueller recalled when SpaceX got its first rocket engine running, and when that engine exploded, when another rocket crashed before "finally" making a successful launch to orbit in 2008 "It's just been an incredible ride," he said. Mueller left SpaceX in 2020 and maintains a considerable financial interest in the firm.SpaceX chief operating officer Gwynne Shotwell told CNBC she was not sure the company would go public, but it "feels like the right time now"."We've been feeling over the last few years a lot of pressure from everyday Americans and our friends that wanted to buy stock," she told the US business news network.Shotwell said SpaceX has long-term ambitions and she does not want to focus on quarterly results."I'm not saying we're not going to do right by our investors, but what folks that invest in SpaceX need to know... is that what we're doing is very futuristic."The listing on the technology-focused Nasdaq index is being viewed by some as a test case for other companies with private valuations nearing $1tn, including Anthropic and OpenAI.Becoming a public company will lead to more scrutiny, but Musk will maintain almost total control. Despite SpaceX becoming a public company, which will put its operations under more scrutiny, Musk will maintain almost total control of it.He will maintain roughly 40% of SpaceX total equity through his combined holding of different types of shares, which come with extra voting rights meaning he effectively controls 85% of the company.With so much control consolidated with Musk, SpaceX will not even need to have on its board of directors anyone considered to be "independent" - someone who does not have a direct personal or financial interest in the company.Such control creates potential risk for investors, according to analysis from Harvard Law School, because SpaceX insiders will be able to make decisions on business deals, including possible acquisitions of other Musk-owned entities, as well as his compensation.Already, SpaceX has acquired Musk's startup xAI, which itself acquired the social media platform X in 2025. Musk had bought the platform formerly known as Twitter in 2022.Asked about the governance structure, chief operating officer Shotwell told CNBC: "There is no-one who can run this company other than Elon, frankly. We want Elon to have that kind of control."Musk has divided public opinion in recent years, using his power and wealth in controversial ways.He helped fund Donald Trump's second run for office before their relationship imploded, secured billions in US government contracts and has dabbled in the internal affairs and politics of other countries.His posts and comments about matters in the UK, Germany and other European states have frequently angered politicians. Sometimes they appeared to have come at a cost to his businesses.
contentParagraphs.zh: 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的 SpaceX 在全球最大的股票上市前筹集了 750 亿美元 29 分钟前，科技记者卡利·海斯 (Kali Hays) 和商业记者杰玛·克鲁 (Jemma Crew) 盖蒂图片社 SpaceX 在周五成为上市公司之前，已从金融公司筹集了 750 亿美元（560 亿英镑），预计这将是历史上价值最高的股票上市。 在向美国证券交易委员会提交的文件中，这家太空探索和人工智能 (AI) 公司表示，它已经出售了每股价值 750 亿美元的股票，售价为 135 美元。股价与 SpaceX 上周给出的估计相符，该公司预计初始股票市值将接近 1.8 兆美元。按照这个价值，首席执行官埃隆·马斯克（已经是世界首富）将成为世界上第一位万亿富翁。SpaceX 最初只出售该公司现有股票总数的 5%。一旦这些股票开始交易，其价值可能会上涨或下跌，具体取决于可供出售的股票数量如果周五开盘时该公司的股价达到或超过 135 美元，SpaceX 将立即成为世界上最有价值的上市公司之一。然而，他们认为这些股票是否值这么多钱，是由投资者决定的。投资基金和个人（通常被称为“散户投资者”）对收购 SpaceX 股份的兴趣越来越高。某些金融分析师已经将 SpaceX 股票的目标价格设定为高于 135 美元的预估，其中包括全球券商 Oppenheimer，该公司周四表示，预计该公司将达到每股 190 美元。该公司股票的公开价格最终通过本质上是公开股票市场上的拍卖。佩塔·库珀 (Peta Cooper) 佩塔·库珀 (Peta Cooper) 对投资 SpaceXP 感到兴奋。佩塔·库珀 (Peta Cooper) 现年 43 岁，是一名住在康沃尔郡的文案撰稿人，她是计划购买 SpaceX 股票的人之一。库珀在加利福尼亚州长大，已经拥有主要投资于科技和加密公司的股票投资组合，计划投资约 750 英镑。“这真的很令人兴奋。我真的很喜欢航天工业。[SpaceX] 迄今为止在发射和创新方面拥有非常出色的记录，”她说。虽然她预计股价在某个时候会下跌，但她计划长期持有这只股票。“我计划将其保留在我的投资组合中，并让它增长。”许多人将今天的 SpaceX IPO 视为对人工智能的一次巨大赌注，一些分析师对此表示怀疑。曾在 NASA 工作的经济学家西尼德·奥沙利文 (Sinead O'Sullivan) 表示，“这是一场巨大的骰子游戏”，“一家公司有很多东西，一个股价我相信埃隆·马斯克擅长执行技术创新吗？是的。我相信股价代表了他将要创造的未来价值吗？可能不会……”SpaceX 的第一位正式员工、现任 Impulse Space 创始人的汤姆·穆勒 (Tom Mueller) 告诉 BBC 的米歇尔·弗勒里 (Michelle Fleury)，看到该公司的发展“令人难以置信”。穆勒回忆起 SpaceX 的第一台火箭发动机运行时以及该发动机爆炸时的情况。 “终于”在 2008 年成功发射进入轨道“这是一次令人难以置信的旅程，”他说。穆勒于 2020 年离开 SpaceX，并在该公司保持着相当大的财务利益。SpaceX 首席运营官格温·肖特韦尔 (Gwynne Shotwell) 告诉 CNBC，她不确定该公司是否会上市，但“感觉现在正是时候”。她对美国商业新闻网表示，“过去几年，我们感受到了来自普通美国人和我们想要购买股票的朋友的很大压力。”肖特韦尔表示，SpaceX 有长期雄心，她不想专注于季度业绩“我并不是说我们不会对投资者做正确的事，但投资 SpaceX 的人需要知道的是……我们正在做的事情非常具有未来感。”一些人将在以技术为重点的纳斯达克指数上市视为对其他私人估值接近 1 兆美元的公司的测试案例，包括 Anthropic 和 OpenAI。成为上市公司将导致更多审查，但马斯克将保持几乎完全控制权。尽管 SpaceX 成为一家上市公司，其运营将受到更严格的审查，但马斯克仍将保持对它的几乎全部控制权。他将通过合并持有不同类型的股票，维持 SpaceX 总股本的大约 40%，这意味着他实际上控制着公司 85% 的股份。这意味着他实际上控制着公司 85% 的股份。由于与马斯克合并了如此多的控制权，SpaceX 甚至不需要在其董事会中拥有任何被认为是“独立”的人——即在公司中没有直接个人或经济利益的人。哈佛法学院的分析显示，这种控制权给投资者带来了潜在风险，因为 SpaceX 内部人士将能够就商业交易做出决定，包括可能收购马斯克旗下的其他实体，以及他的薪酬。SpaceX 已经收购了马斯克的初创公司 xAI，后者本身于 2025 年收购了社交媒体平台 X。马斯克在 2022 年收购了该平台（以前称为 Twitter）。在被问及治理结构时，首席运营官肖特韦尔告诉 CNBC：“坦率地说，除了埃隆，我们希望埃隆能够拥有这种控制权。近年来，马斯克以有争议的方式利用自己的权力和财富，引发了公众舆论的分裂。在他们的关系破裂之前，他帮助资助了唐纳德·特朗普的第二次竞选，获得了数十亿美元的美国政府合同，并涉足其他国家的内政和政治。他对英国、德国和其他欧洲国家事务的帖子和评论经常激怒政客。有时，这些举措似乎让他的企业付出了代价。

articles.title: New study finds 'foundation' species continue to shape ecosystems after their death
articles.summary: Foundation species like coral, oysters and big trees are critical to their ecosystems, providing food and shelter. A new study finds their influence continues after their death.
articles.titleZh: 新研究发现“基础”物种死亡后继续塑造生态系统
articles.summaryZh: 珊瑚、牡蛎和大树等基础物种对其生态系统至关重要，提供食物和庇护所。一项新的研究发现，他们的影响力在他们死后仍在继续。

contentParagraphs.en: In another whiplash decision, President Trump announced yesterday that he canceled new attacks in Iran, after announcing earlier in the day that more strikes were coming if the country didn't agree to a peace deal. The president signaled that the two countries had "conceptually" secured an agreement on nuclear issues and could sign a peace deal in the next few days, but Iran's foreign ministry says a deal hasn't been finalized.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在另一项重大决定中，特朗普总统昨天宣布，他取消了对伊朗的新袭击，此前他在当天早些时候宣布，如果伊朗不同意和平协议，将会发动更多袭击。总统表示，两国已“概念上”就核问题达成协议，并可能在未来几天内签署和平协议，但伊朗外交部表示，协议尚未敲定。

contentParagraphs.en: A small motorboat passes anchored vessels in the Strait of Hormuz off Bandar Abbas, Iran, on June 11.
contentParagraphs.zh: 6 月 11 日，伊朗阿巴斯港附近的霍尔木兹海峡，一艘小型摩托艇驶过停泊的船只。

contentParagraphs.en: Amirhosein Khorgooi/ISNA via AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿米尔侯赛因·霍尔古伊/ISNA 图片来源：美联社

contentParagraphs.en: Amirhosein Khorgooi/ISNA via AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿米尔侯赛因·霍尔古伊/ISNA 图片来源：美联社

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 Trump wants this war to end, NPR's Aya Batrawy tells Up First. It's unpopular among Americans, who saw inflation surge to its highest level in several years. Arab Gulf countries are also hurting as the war drags on. Batrawy says a call between Trump and Qatar's emir yesterday, in which messages from Iran were exchanged, could have led Trump to call off more attacks. The United Arab Emirates has also shifted its tone from being hawkish on Iran to aligning more with Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt about ending the war. That leaves Israel as the only country railing against a diplomatic approach.
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Aya Batrawy 告诉 Up First，特朗普希望结束这场战争。它在美国人中不受欢迎，因为他们的通货膨胀率飙升至几年来的最高水平。随着战争的持续，阿拉伯海湾国家也受到了伤害。巴特拉维表示，特朗普与卡塔尔埃米尔昨天通话，交换了来自伊朗的信息，这可能会导致特朗普取消更多袭击。阿拉伯联合酋长国也改变了对伊朗的强硬态度，转而在结束战争问题上与沙特阿拉伯、土耳其和埃及结盟。这使得以色列成为唯一反对外交途径的国家。

contentParagraphs.en: Trump has nominated Jay Clayton to serve as director of national intelligence. Clayton is a federal prosecutor and former head of the Securities and Exchange Commission. His nomination comes after Trump named his close political ally Bill Pulte to serve as acting director of national intelligence. Pulte was appointed despite not having experience in national security, and his selection sparked a political backlash that doomed Congress' efforts to renew a crucial intelligence tool that is set to expire today. The tool, called FISA 702, allows the government to collect the electronic communications of foreign nationals located outside of the U.S.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普提名杰伊·克莱顿担任国家情报总监。克莱顿是联邦检察官和证券交易委员会前主席。在特朗普任命他的亲密政治盟友比尔·普尔特担任代理国家情报总监之后，他获得了提名。尽管普尔特在国家安全方面没有经验，但他还是被任命了，他的选择引发了政治反弹，导致国会更新一项将于今天到期的重要情报工具的努力失败。该工具名为 FISA 702，允许政府收集位于美国境外的外国人的电子通信信息。

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 NPR's Eric McDaniel says that the tool's expiration is symbolically — but not practically — a big deal. Symbolically, McDaniel says it shows that the president makes decisions that make Congress' job harder. He could have waited until the tool was renewed to announce Pulte as acting national intelligence director. But practically, the spy tool can still be used for many months under the existing court authorization.
contentParagraphs.zh: 🎧 NPR 的埃里克·麦克丹尼尔 (Eric McDaniel) 表示，该工具的过期具有象征意义，但实际上并不重要。麦克丹尼尔表示，具有象征意义的是，这表明总统做出的决定会让国会的工作变得更加困难。他本可以等到该工具更新后才宣布普尔特担任代理国家情报总监。但实际上，根据现有的法院授权，该间谍工具仍然可以使用数月。

contentParagraphs.en: The Trump administration is trying to downsize the U.S. Forest Service and eliminate wildfire and smoke research, just as the American West faces a potentially epic summer fire season. The administration has identified 90 research stations for closure as part of its Forest Service reorganization plan, which includes relocating its headquarters from Washington, D.C., to Utah and consolidating regional offices into individual state facilities. U.S. Forest Service Chief Tom Schultz has defended the proposed reorganization, noting it has been considered by previous administrations since 2006. If Congress approves Trump's proposed budget for the agency, the U.S. Forest Service would be a skeleton of its former self, just as climate change is accelerating the frequency and severity of wildfires.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普政府正试图缩小美国林务局的规模，并取消野火和烟雾研究，就像美国西部面临着潜在的史诗般的夏季火灾季节一样。政府已确定关闭 90 个研究站，作为其林务局重组计划的一部分，其中包括将其总部从华盛顿特区迁至犹他州，并将地区办事处合并为单独的州设施。美国林务局局长汤姆·舒尔茨为重组提议进行了辩护，并指出自 2006 年以来历届政府都曾考虑过这一重组。如果国会批准特朗普为该机构提出的预算，美国林务局将只剩下前身的骨架，就像气候变化正在加速野火的频率和严重程度一样。

contentParagraphs.en: The U.S. men's national soccer team takes on Paraguay today in its first group-stage match of the 2026 World Cup. It's the team's long-awaited chance to finally rewrite a legacy of inferiority in international soccer. The furthest the U.S. men's team has advanced in a modern World Cup was to the quarterfinals in 2002. Since then, the team has managed just three total wins across all World Cups. This year, as the U.S. hosts the tournament, the team has gathered what may be the most talented generation of players American soccer has ever produced. Here's what to know about each player on the team.
contentParagraphs.zh: 今天，美国国家男子足球队将在 2026 年世界杯小组赛首场比赛中迎战巴拉圭队。这是该队期待已久的机会，可以最终改写国际足坛的劣根性。美国男队在现代世界杯上晋级最远的一次是 2002 年进入四分之一决赛。此后，该队在所有世界杯上仅取得了 3 场胜利。今年，随着美国主办这项赛事，该队聚集了可能是美国足球有史以来最有才华的一代球员。以下是关于球队中每位球员的信息。

contentParagraphs.en: Shayanta Chowdhury
contentParagraphs.zh: 沙彦塔·乔杜里

contentParagraphs.en: The northern bald ibis vanished from Europe around 400 years ago due to overhunting. A century ago, scientists discovered another population of these birds in Syria and Morocco and decided to bring some to Europe to rear their chicks in captivity. In the fall of 2024, photographer Gunnar Hartmann joined the conservation and research group Waldrappteam for 50 days as they flew an ultralight aircraft across southern Germany, France and Spain to assist the ibises with their migration. While in Spain, Hartmann captured an image that won this year's Scientist at Work photography competition, sponsored by the journal Nature. The winners were announced on Wednesday. In the stunning photo, the aircraft glides beneath a yellow parachute, while 19 of the birds fly ahead, following the people guiding them. Take a look at the photo and others that won in the competition.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大约 400 年前，由于过度捕猎，北方秃鹮从欧洲消失了。一个世纪前，科学家在叙利亚和摩洛哥发现了这些鸟类的另一个种群，并决定将其中一些带到欧洲圈养它们的雏鸟。 2024 年秋天，摄影师 Gunnar Hartmann 加入了 Waldrapp 保护和研究小组，为期 50 天，他们驾驶超轻型飞机穿越德国南部、法国和西班牙，协助朱鹮迁徙。在西班牙期间，哈特曼拍摄的一张照片赢得了今年由《自然》杂志赞助的科学家在职摄影比赛。获奖者于周三公布。在这张令人惊叹的照片中，飞机在黄色降落伞下滑行，而 19 只鸟则跟随引导它们的人们飞在前面。看看照片和其他在比赛中获胜的照片。

articles.title: Pokémon Go data trained AI that could assist military drones in war zones
articles.summary:
Location scans from the globally popular augmented reality game have helped train AI to recognise and interpret physical spaces

An AI model trained on data collec
articles.titleZh: Pokémon Go 经过数据训练的人工智能可以协助战区的军用无人机
articles.summaryZh:
全球流行的增强现实游戏的位置扫描有助于训练人工智能识别和解释物理空间

关注我们的澳大利亚新闻直播博客以获取最新动态

获取我们的突发新闻电子邮件、免费应用程序或每日新闻播客

基于数据收集训练的人工智能模型

contentParagraphs.en: An AI model trained on data collected from users of Pokémon Go will potentially help military drones find their location in war zones.Pokémon Go, a 2016 augmented reality mobile game, allowed players to find and catch Pokémon in the real world using the cameras on their mobile phones, and exploded in popularity. In 2018, the company reported having more than 800m downloads worldwide.A 2021 update to the game introduced Pokéstops, which gave players in-game rewards for scanning real locations using their devices. It required users to opt in and upload the recording.Niantic, which created Pokémon in partnership with Nintendo, collected users’ location scan data before the company sold its gaming division in 2025.The historical scans were used to train the company’s AI models to recognise and interpret spaces in the physical world, as first reported by DroneXL this week.Sign up for the Breaking News Australia emailNiantic Spatial – a spin-off company from Niantic – announced its partnership with Vantor, a company that specialises in spatial detection software for drones, including those used by some militaries, in December.The agreement is designed to allow drones to navigate and coordinate precisely in areas where GPS is not available.“The partnership addresses a critical vulnerability in modern operations: GPS unavailability, spoofing, interference, and jamming,” the announcement stated. “When satellite signals are compromised, autonomous systems and field teams lose their ability to orient, coordinate or maintain accurate situational awareness.”Vantor’s chief product officer, Peter Wilczynski, highlighted the benefits of the two companies working together in a December interview with Tectonic Defence.“The modern battle space is going to be complete with different systems, and you’re going to want to upgrade those systems quickly—bringing new hardware online faster than new software,” Wilczynski said.Both companies told Guardian Australia that ground scans from the game were not provided to Vantor as part of the partnership, but the scans from Pokémon Go were used to train Niantic’s foundation models.“AR Scans collected through Pokémon Go were submitted voluntarily by players who opted into the feature and were subject to the applicable Terms of Service and Privacy Policy at the time,” the Niantic Spatial spokesperson said.Both companies said the partnership was still in its early stages.Tom Sulston, head of policy for tech policy think tank Digital Rights Watch said the use of civilian data for military ends was troubling.“While they may have disclaimers in their Ts&Cs, we know that most people don’t read vast legal documents when they want to play a video game,” he said.“We need regulators to focus on ‘best interests of the user’ or ‘fair and reasonable’ tests to keep users safe from exploitation like this.“While we’re waiting for the government to catch up, it’s important that we remember that ‘free’ software services often treat the user not as a customer but as the product to be sold.”Dr Rob Nicholls, senior researcher associated at the University of Sydney’s centre for AI, trust and governance, said the case was likely the tip of the iceberg regarding data collected from apps being used for other purposes.“We have already seen that Strava data has been used to identify the location of military facilities,” he said. “Indeed, directives not to use devices with GPS and sharing have come from a number of different militaries.”Vantor announced in February a deal with the US Army of up to US$217m for training software.Niantic sold its video game division to Saudi Arabian-owned Scopely for US$3.5bn in 2025.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据 Pokémon Go 用户收集的数据进行训练的人工智能模型将有可能帮助军用无人机在战区找到自己的位置。Pokémon Go 是一款 2016 年的增强现实手机游戏，允许玩家使用手机上的摄像头在现实世界中寻找和捕捉 Pokémon，并迅速流行起来。 2018 年，该公司报告全球下载量超过 8 亿次。2021 年游戏更新引入了 Pokéstops，为使用设备扫描真实位置的玩家提供游戏内奖励。它要求用户选择加入并上传录音。与任天堂合作创建神奇宝贝的 Niantic 在该公司于 2025 年出售其游戏部门之前收集了用户的位置扫描数据。正如 DroneXL 本周首次报道的那样，这些历史扫描数据用于训练该公司的人工智能模型来识别和解释物理世界中的空间。 12 月，无人机空间探测软件，包括一些军队使用的无人机。该协议旨在让无人机在 GPS 不可用的区域进行精确导航和协调。“该合作伙伴关系解决了现代操作中的一个关键漏洞：GPS 不可用、欺骗、干扰和干扰，”公告称。 “当卫星信号受到损害时，自主系统和现场团队就失去了定向、协调或保持准确态势感知的能力。”Vantor 的首席产品官 Peter Wilczynski 在 12 月接受 Tectonic Defence 采访时强调了两家公司合作的好处。Wilczynski 表示：“现代战场空间将由不同的系统组成，你会希望快速升级这些系统，新硬件上线的速度比新软件更快。”作为合作伙伴关系的一部分，游戏中的地面扫描数据并未提供给 Vantor，但 Pokémon Go 的扫描数据被用来训练 Niantic 的基础模型。Niantic Spatial 发言人表示：“通过 Pokémon Go 收集的 AR 扫描数据是由选择该功能的玩家自愿提交的，并受到当时适用的服务条款和隐私政策的约束。”两家公司均表示，合作关系仍处于早期阶段。技术政策政策主管 Tom Sulston 认为Tank Digital Rights Watch 表示，将民用数据用于军事目的令人不安。“虽然他们可能在条款和条件中包含免责声明，但我们知道大多数人在玩视频游戏时不会阅读大量法律文件，”他说。“我们需要监管机构关注‘用户的最大利益’或‘公平合理’的测试，以确保用户免受此类剥削。在我们等待政府迎头赶上的同时，重要的是我们要记住‘免费’悉尼大学人工智能、信任和治理中心的高级研究员 Rob Nicholls 博士表示，该案件很可能只是从应用程序收集的数据被用于其他目的的冰山一角。 Vantor 在 2 月份宣布与美国陆军达成一项价值高达 2.17 亿美元的训练软件协议。Niantic 于 2025 年以 35 亿美元的价格将其视频游戏部门出售给沙特阿拉伯旗下的 Scopely。
