articles.title: This week's Short Wave news roundup
articles.summary: NPR's Short Wave team talks about how air pollution affects the brain, what ancient squirrel poop reveals about prehistoric fauna, and a whale graveyard on the ocean floor.
articles.titleZh: 本周短波新闻综述
articles.summaryZh: NPR 的短波团队讨论了空气污染如何影响大脑、古代松鼠粪便揭示的史前动物群以及海底的鲸鱼墓地。

contentParagraphs.en: NPR's Short Wave team talks about how air pollution affects the brain, what ancient squirrel poop reveals about prehistoric fauna, and a whale graveyard on the ocean floor.
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的短波团队讨论了空气污染如何影响大脑、古代松鼠粪便揭示的史前动物群以及海底的鲸鱼墓地。

contentParagraphs.en: MARY LOUISE KELLY, HOST: It is time for our science news roundup from Short Wave. That is NPR's science podcast. I am joined by two members of the team, Angela Zhang and Emily Kwong. Hi, you two.EMILY KWONG, BYLINE: Hi, Mary Louise.ANGELA ZHANG, BYLINE: Hello. Great to be here.KELLY: Hi. So y'all have, as you always do, kindly brought us three science stories that caught your attention this week. What are they?ZHANG: Well, the first is how air pollution might be making your memory worse.KWONG: The second is the discovery of an ancient whale graveyard.ZHANG: And finally, what prehistoric mysteries are revealed by ancient squirrel poop.KELLY: Ancient (laughter).KWONG: A time capsule. You'll...KELLY: Wow. We have a lot of ancient animals going here. Let's start with the serious one - air pollution and memory?ZHANG: So the study I have for you today is about these really, really tiny particles of air pollution, like 30 times smaller than the width of a human hair. And these particles are released by wildfires and car exhaust among other things, and they're bad for your heart and your lungs, and they can also get directly to the brain through the blood.KELLY: That sounds awful. What do we know about how they affect, say, a person's memory, though?KWONG: Yeah, so to study how air pollution affects the brain, researchers used a database containing information about Black Americans living in California. The researchers looked at how much air pollution someone may have experienced based on their home address and then looked at their cognitive test scores. And while we know pollution is bad for the brain in general, what the researchers found is that it also affected a specific type of memory.KATHRYN CONLON: The people who had been exposed to more pollution over the years had weaker semantic memory. So really, that long-term pollution looked like it was aging the brain's memory ahead of schedule.ZHANG: That's Kathryn Conlon, a study author. She's a professor of epidemiology at the University of California, Davis. She told me that semantic memory is how your brain stores information, so like that New Delhi is the capital of India or that three plus two equals five.CONLON: When that's affected, we can think about how that's the kind of thing that might chip away at a person's independence or ability to have the quality of life that we would hope to have.KELLY: But how do they know it's air pollution and not some other factor in these people's lives?KWONG: The researchers did control for differences in education and income. But when it comes to geography, you're right - it is hard to separate air pollution from things like noise exposure, which could also influence brain health. They can't say for sure that it's air pollution that is leading to worse memory.ZHANG: But including Black Americans in research, which is what the study did, means that solutions can hopefully reduce inequity, since they're almost twice as likely as white Americans to have dementia and they're more likely to live in polluted or redline neighborhoods.KELLY: OK, let's turn to topic Number 2. From up in the air to under the sea, we are going to a whale graveyard. I'm trying to picture this, Emily.KWONG: Yeah, it's a really cool place. So when whales die, many fall to the bottom of the sea, and when they land, their carcasses become an energy-rich habitat, drawing a wide variety of organisms from across the deep sea to feast. Scientists call these sites whale falls.KELLY: Whale falls because they're fallen whales. So...KWONG: Yes.KELLY: Now I'm picturing, like, a deep-sea buffet for all these (laughter) way deep-down creatures.KWONG: Yeah, who are used to just getting, like, snacks drifting down to the bottom. So it's really cool. And a research team in China may have found the deepest and most extensive whale graveyard in the world, located in the Diamantina zone in the Indian Ocean. The team published these findings in the journal Nature.KELLY: And what does this whale graveyard actually look like?ZHANG: Yeah, so you can picture a nearly 750-mile long stretch of whale fossils. These are bones that are shaped like chimneys or even giant cigars. And the oldest fossils were 5.3 million years old. It's pretty old.KWONG: So old. And during dives with a submersible, the team also discovered five of those whale falls, these five whale carcasses teeming with scavengers. The team saw a whole thicket of microbes, squat lobsters, brittle stars and jellyfish and even species that may be new to science, like bone-eating worms.KELLY: Bone-eating worms. OK, Emily, you just said this is the Indian Ocean that we're in, right? Is there something specific about that, Angela, that so many whale carcasses end up in the - this particular part of the Indian Ocean?ZHANG: One reason offered by the lead author Xiaotong Peng, based at the Chinese Academy of Sciences, may have to do with the shape of the sea floor in this part of the world or maybe even the currents that are around it.XIAOTONG PENG: The surface currents and the deep currents can funnel or concentrate those carcass on the sea floor.KWONG: Biologists, by the way, are so excited about this finding because these ecosystems are incredibly unique. Stephen Godfrey, a paleontologist at the Calvert Marine Museum, who was not a part of the paper, said the study reminded him of a trailer for the first in a series of epic movies and that he hopes there will be many more of these blockbusters to come.KELLY: Hear, hear. OK, that brings us to our last story. You mentioned prehistoric mysteries. You mentioned ancient squirrel poop.ZHANG: (Laughter).KELLY: Where is this...KWONG: We got the goods.KELLY: Where is this going, Angela?ZHANG: All right, so we got to go back in time to the Pleistocene Epoch. This is one of the most critica
contentParagraphs.zh: 主持人玛丽·路易丝·凯利：现在是短波科学新闻综述的时间了。这是 NPR 的科学播客。与我一起的还有团队的两名成员：张安琪 (Angela Chang) 和邝艾米丽 (Emily Kwong)。大家好。邝美莉连线：嗨，玛丽·路易丝。张安琪连线：你好。很高兴来到这里。 凯莉：嗨。所以，你们大家一如既往地为我们带来了本周引起你们注意的三个科学故事。张：嗯，第一个是空气污染如何让你的记忆力变差。邝：第二个是古代鲸鱼墓地的发现。张：最后，古代松鼠的粪便揭示了哪些史前之谜。凯莉：古代（笑声）。邝：一个时间胶囊。你会... 凯莉：哇。我们有很多古老的动物来到这里。让我们从严重的问题开始——空气污染和记忆？ 张：所以我今天给你们做的研究是关于这些非常非常小的空气污染颗粒，比人类头发丝的宽度小30倍。这些颗粒是由野火和汽车尾气等物质释放的，它们对你的心脏和肺部有害，而且它们还可以通过血液直接进入大脑。 凯利：这听起来很糟糕。然而，我们对它们如何影响人的记忆了解多少？ 邝：是的，为了研究空气污染如何影响大脑，研究人员使用了一个包含居住在加利福尼亚州的美国黑人信息的数据库。研究人员根据某人的家庭住址观察了他们可能经历过的空气污染程度，然后观察了他们的认知测试分数。虽然我们知道污染总体上对大脑有害，但研究人员发现，它还会影响特定类型的记忆。 凯瑟琳·康伦：多年来接触更多污染的人语义记忆较弱。确实，长期污染看起来正在提前老化大脑的记忆。 张：这是凯瑟琳·康伦，一位研究作者。她是加州大学戴维斯分校的流行病学教授。她告诉我，语义记忆是你的大脑存储信息的方式，就像新德里是印度的首都或者三加二等于五一样。 CONLON：当它受到影响时，我们可以考虑这种事情会如何削弱一个人的独立性或获得我们希望的生活质量的能力。KELLY：但是他们怎么知道这是空气污染，而不是这些人生活中的其他因素？ KWONG：研究人员确实控制了教育差异和收入。但说到地理，你是对的——很难将空气污染与噪音等因素区分开来，噪音也可能影响大脑健康。他们不能肯定地说是空气污染导致了记忆力下降。 张：但是，将美国黑人纳入研究中，这就是这项研究所做的，意味着解决方案有望减少不平等，因为他们患痴呆症的可能性几乎是美国白人的两倍，而且他们更有可能生活在污染或红线社区。 凯利：好的，让我们转向第二个主题。从空中到海底，我们将前往鲸鱼墓地。我正在努力想象这个情景，Emily。 KWONG：是的，那是一个非常酷的地方。因此，当鲸鱼死亡时，许多鲸鱼会落入海底，当它们登陆时，它们的尸体就变成了能源丰富的栖息地，吸引了来自深海的各种生物体来享用。科学家将这些地点称为鲸鱼瀑布。 凯利：鲸鱼瀑布是因为它们是坠落的鲸鱼。所以... KWONG： 是的。 KELLY： 现在我正在想象，就像是所有这些（笑声）深处生物的深海自助餐。 KWONG： 是的，他们习惯于吃零食漂到海底。所以这真的很酷。中国的一个研究小组可能发现了世界上最深、最广泛的鲸鱼墓地，位于印度洋的迪亚曼蒂纳地区。研究小组在《自然》杂志上发表了这些发现。 凯利：这个鲸鱼墓地实际上是什么样子？ 张：是的，所以你可以想象出一条近 750 英里长的鲸鱼化石。这些骨头的形状像烟囱，甚至像巨型雪茄。最古老的化石已有 530 万年的历史。相当旧了。 邝：好旧了。在潜水器潜水期间，研究小组还发现了五个鲸鱼瀑布，这五个鲸鱼尸体上充满了食腐动物。研究小组看到了一大堆微生物、矮胖龙虾、海蛇尾和水母，甚至还有一些对科学来说可能是新物种，比如食骨蠕虫。凯利：食骨蠕虫。好吧，艾米丽，你刚才说这是我们所在的印度洋，对吧？安吉拉，这么多鲸鱼的尸体最后都流到了印度洋的这个特定区域，这有什么特别的吗？ 张：主要作者、中国科学院的彭晓彤提出的一个原因可能与该地区海底的形状，甚至可能与周围的洋流有关。彭晓彤：表层洋流和深海洋流可以将这些鲸鱼尸体汇集或集中在海底。邝：顺便说一句，生物学家对这一发现非常兴奋，因为这些生态系统非常独特。卡尔弗特海洋博物馆的古生物学家斯蒂芬·戈弗雷（Stephen Godfrey）并未参与该论文，他说这项研究让他想起了一系列史诗电影中第一部的预告片，他希望未来能有更多这样的大片。 凯利：听，听。好的，这就是我们最后一个故事。你提到了史前之谜。你提到了古代松鼠的粪便。 张：（笑声）。 凯利： 这是哪里...邝： 我们拿到货了。 凯利： 这是要去哪里，安吉拉？ 张： 好吧，所以我们必须回到更新世。这是最受批评的之一

articles.title: New Zealand great Kane Williamson retires from international cricket
articles.summary: New Zealand's most prolific run-scorer and arguably greatest-ever batsman announces immediate retirement.
articles.titleZh: 新西兰伟大的凯恩威廉姆森从国际板球比赛退役
articles.summaryZh: 新西兰最多产的跑动得分手和可以说是有史以来最伟大的击球手宣布立即退役。

contentParagraphs.en: New Zealand’s most prolific run-scorer and arguably greatest-ever batsman announces immediate retirement.Former New Zealand ‌captain Kane Williamson has announced his retirement from international cricket, bringing ⁠an end to a 16-year career marked by numerous accolades and by captaining his side to the title in ⁠the inaugural World Test Championship in 2021.The 35-year-old batsman made his debut in the longest format against India in November 2010 ⁠in Ahmedabad, and played 110 tests in all, scoring 9,515 runs at an average of 54.06.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Jose Mourinho named Real Madrid coach on three-year deallist 2 of 4Somali referee banned by US to officiate European Super Cup, UEFA sayslist 3 of 4UFC 250: Topuria-Gaethje blockbuster to take place in front of US presidentlist 4 of 4World celebrates, but Gaza watches the World Cup from a distanceend of listHe is New Zealand’s all-time leading international run-scorer with 19,346 runs, including 48 centuries and six double-centuries across all ‌formats. He will no longer play a part in the ongoing test series against England.“I’ve always felt a strong drive and hunger for international cricket, and I take pride in knowing I’ve given it my all in every match I’ve played for New Zealand,” Williamson said in a statement on Friday.“Continuing with anything less wouldn’t ⁠be right, and I feel fortunate to step ⁠away on my own terms.“I leave feeling optimistic about where this group is heading. There’s a huge amount of talent, and a real desire to do something ⁠special with this New Zealand team.”Williamson led the Kiwis as captain across formats between 2016 ⁠and 2024. He led the test side ⁠for 40 matches and guided them to the title in the 2021 World Test Championship, beating India in the final.He won 22 tests and scored 11 hundreds as ‌captain before stepping down from the role in 2022.Williamson’s list of accolades includes being named the ICC Cricketer of 2015 and Test Player ‌of 2019, while also winning the Sir Richard Hadlee Medal on a record four occasions.
contentParagraphs.zh: 新西兰最多产的跑动得分手和可以说是有史以来最伟大的击球手宣布立即退役。前新西兰队队长凯恩·威廉姆森宣布从国际板球比赛退役，结束了他 16 年获得无数荣誉的职业生涯，并在 2021 年首届世界测试锦标赛中带领球队夺得冠军。这位 35 岁的击球手在最长的比赛中首次亮相2010 年 11 月，印度在艾哈迈达巴德进行了 110 场测试，得分 9,515 分，平均分 54.06。 推荐故事列表 4 项列表 1 of 4 何塞·穆里尼奥在三年交易列表中任命皇马教练 4 中的 2 索马里裁判被美国禁止主持欧洲超级杯，欧足联表示列表 3 of 4UFC 250：托普里亚-加瑟杰的重磅炸弹将在美国总统名单上的 4 of 4World 庆祝，但加沙从名单的最远处观看世界杯。他是新西兰历史上领先的国际跑得分手，共 19,346 次跑动，其中包括 48 个世纪和 6 个跨世纪的所有赛制。他将不再参加正在进行的对阵英格兰的测试系列赛。威廉姆森在周五的一份声明中表示：“我一直对国际板球运动有强烈的动力和渴望，我很自豪知道我在为新西兰队效力的每场比赛中都竭尽全力。”新西兰队才华横溢，并且真正渴望与这支新西兰队一起做一些特别的事情。”威廉姆森在 2016 年至 2024 年期间以队长身份带领新西兰队完成了 40 场比赛，并带领他们在 2021 年世界测试锦标赛中夺得冠军，并在决赛中击败了印度队。在辞去队长一职之前，他赢得了 22 场测试并获得了 1100 分。 2022.威廉姆森的荣誉清单包括被评为 2015 年国际板球运动员和 2019 年测试球员，同时还创纪录地四次赢得理查德·哈德利爵士奖章。

articles.title: Canadian mother sues OpenAI, alleging ChatGPT led her daughter to kill herself
articles.summary:
Suit filed in US alleges chatbot told Alice Carrier, 24, ‘maybe this is just the end’ as she struggled with suicidal thoughts
A Canadian mother sued OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, in US court on Thursday, alleging that ChatGPT encouraged her daughter to kill herself. The lawsuit
articles.titleZh: 加拿大母亲起诉 OpenAI，指控 ChatGPT 导致女儿自杀
articles.summaryZh:
在美国提起的诉讼称，当 24 岁的爱丽丝·卡里尔 (Alice Carrier) 与自杀念头作斗争时，聊天机器人告诉她“也许这只是结局”
周四，一位加拿大母亲在美国法院起诉 OpenAI 及其首席执行官 Sam Altman，指控 ChatGPT 鼓励她的女儿自杀。诉讼

contentParagraphs.en: A Canadian mother sued OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, in US court on Thursday, alleging that ChatGPT encouraged her daughter to kill herself. The lawsuit is the latest in a slew accusing the company of failing to address dangerous conversations between users and the company’s chatbot.Kristie Carrier said in a lawsuit filed in San Francisco state court that her daughter, Alice, told ChatGPT about her suicidal ideations more than a dozen times leading up to her death but that OpenAI’s safety systems never flagged the conversations for human review or terminated them.“ChatGPT took on the persona of a confidant, a best friend, a therapist at times, even though it was not capable of safely and responsibly engaging in this way with my child,” Carrier said in a statement.OpenAI has said it trains its models to direct people who express intent to harm themselves to seek help and connect with real-world resources.“This is a heartbreaking situation and our thoughts are with everyone impacted. We’re currently reviewing the legal filing, which indicates that these interactions took place on an earlier version of ChatGPT that is no longer available,” said Drew Pusateri, a spokesperson for OpenAI.The platform initially told Alice Carrier to seek help from a crisis hotline or emergency services. But as OpenAI updated ChatGPT to make its responses sound more human, her interactions with the platform deepened, with Alice Carrier sharing more personal information and ChatGPT responding in ways that mimicked a friend or therapist, the lawsuit said.The lawsuit claims the chatbot criticized Alice Carrier’s partner and crisis hotlines, validated her suicidal thoughts, and urged her to keep speaking with it. When Alice Carrier said she had suicidal thoughts and had attempted to kill herself, it again suggested a crisis hotline, the lawsuit said.Alice Carrier was working as a web developer in Montreal when she began using ChatGPT in 2023 to troubleshoot problems with computers and gaming consoles, according to the lawsuit.The following year, her relationship with the platform changed, when she turned to ChatGPT with questions about what to do with her suicidal thoughts, as well as suicide methods.Alice Carrier said crisis hotlines were not helpful, and ChatGPT echoed those statements, according to the filing.“Maybe this is just the end,” ChatGPT told her, according to the lawsuit.These events led to Alice Carrier’s suicide last year at the age of 24, her mother alleges.The lawsuit, which accuses OpenAI of negligence in the design of ChatGPT and in its failure to warn users of the product’s dangers, seeks damages and a court order requiring OpenAI to automatically terminate conversations about self-harm and to display warnings about its platform.OpenAI is already facing 18 similar lawsuits filed by families of people who committed or attempted suicide in a coordinated proceeding in California state court, according to lawyers for Kristie Carrier. Google is facing a similar suit over alleged encouragement by its Gemini chatbot.More than 1 million ChatGPT users each week send messages that include “explicit indicators of potential suicidal planning or intent”, according to a blogpost published by OpenAI in October 2025. In addition, OpenAI said that about 0.07% of users active in a given week – about 560,000 of the 800 million weekly users the bot saw then – show “possible signs of mental health emergencies related to psychosis or mania”.“While ChatGPT is not a substitute for medical or mental health care, we have continued to strengthen how it responds in sensitive and acute situations with input from mental health experts. Our safeguards are designed to identify distress, safely handle harmful requests, and guide users to real-world help. This work is ongoing, and we continue to improve it in close consultation with clinicians,” Pusateri, the OpenAI spokesperson, said.Its models are also trained to refuse requests that could “meaningfully enable violence”, and to notify law enforcement when conversations suggest “an imminent and credible risk of harm to others”, with mental health experts helping assess borderline cases, according to OpenAI blogposts.In addition to lawsuits over suicide, the company is facing lawsuits accusing it of assisting school shooters and failing to flag those conversations to law enforcement. Families of seven victims of a mass shooting at a secondary school in British Columbia are suing OpenAI and Altman for negligence after the company failed to alert authorities to the shooter’s troubling conversations with ChatGPT.Florida became the first US state to sue OpenAI earlier this month, accusing the company of harming children by providing information to school shooters, offering guidance on self-harm and addicting young users. The state’s attorney general has also opened a criminal investigation into OpenAI over the chatbot’s alleged role in a shooting.Reuters contributed reporting
contentParagraphs.zh: 周四，一位加拿大母亲在美国法院起诉 OpenAI 及其首席执行官 Sam Altman，指控 ChatGPT 鼓励她的女儿自杀。该诉讼是指控该公司未能解决用户与该公司聊天机器人之间危险对话的一系列诉讼中的最新一起。克里斯蒂·卡里尔 (Kristie Carrier) 在旧金山州法院提起的诉讼中表示，她的女儿爱丽丝 (Alice) 在死亡前多次向 ChatGPT 讲述了她的自杀想法，但 OpenAI 的安全系统从未标记这些对话以供人工审查或终止它们。 Carrier 在一份声明中表示，“有时，即使它无法以这种方式安全、负责任地与我的孩子互动。”OpenAI 表示，它训练其模型，引导那些表达出伤害自己意图的人寻求帮助并与现实世界的资源联系。“这是一个令人心碎的情况，我们的想法与受影响的每个人有关。我们目前正在审查法律文件，该文件表明这些互动是在不再可用的 ChatGPT 早期版本上进行的。” OpenAI 发言人 Pusateri 表示，该平台最初告诉 Alice Carrier 向危机热线或紧急服务部门寻求帮助。但诉讼称，随着 OpenAI 更新了 ChatGPT，使其响应听起来更加人性化，她与平台的互动加深了，Alice Carrier 分享了更多个人信息，而 ChatGPT 以模仿朋友或治疗师的方式做出回应。诉讼称，聊天机器人批评了 Alice Carrier 的伴侣和危机热线，证实了她的自杀想法，并敦促她继续与它交谈。诉讼称，当 Alice Carrier 说她有自杀念头并试图自杀时，它再次暗示了危机热线。诉讼称，2023 年，Alice Carrier 在蒙特利尔担任网络开发人员，她开始使用 ChatGPT 来解决计算机和游戏机的问题。次年，她与该平台的关系发生了变化，当时她向 ChatGPT 询问如何处理自己的自杀想法以及自杀方法。Alice Carrier 表示，危机热线是根据诉讼文件，ChatGPT 告诉她：“也许这只是结束。”她的母亲称，这些事件导致了 Alice Carrier 去年 24 岁的自杀。该诉讼指控 OpenAI 在 ChatGPT 的设计上存在疏忽，未能警告用户该产品的危险，要求赔偿损失，并要求法院命令 OpenAI 自动终止有关该产品的对话。据克里斯蒂·卡里尔 (Kristie Carrier) 的律师称，OpenAI 已经面临 18 起类似诉讼，这些诉讼是由自杀或试图自杀者的家人在加利福尼亚州法院的协调程序中提起的。谷歌也因 Gemini 聊天机器人涉嫌鼓励而面临类似诉讼。根据 OpenAI 2025 年 10 月发布的一篇博文，每周有超过 100 万 ChatGPT 用户发送包含“潜在自杀计划或意图的明确指标”的消息。此外，OpenAI 表示，在某一周内大约有 0.07% 的活跃用户（该机器人当时看到的每周 8 亿用户中约有 56 万）表现出“可能存在自杀迹象”。 OpenAI 发言人 Pusateri 表示：“虽然 ChatGPT 不能替代医疗或心理保健，但我们根据心理健康专家的意见，继续加强其在敏感和紧急情况下的反应方式，旨在识别痛苦、安全处理有害请求，并指导用户获得现实世界的帮助。这项工作正在进行中，我们将与临床医生密切协商，不断改进它。”其模型还经过训练，可以拒绝可能的请求。据 OpenAI 博客文章称，“有意义地促成暴力”，并在对话显示“对他人造成迫在眉睫且可信的伤害风险”时通知执法部门，心理健康专家帮助评估边缘案件。 除了自杀诉讼外，该公司还面临指控其协助校园枪击者并未能向执法部门举报这些对话的诉讼。不列颠哥伦比亚省一所中学大规模枪击事件的七名受害者的家人正在起诉 OpenAI 和 Altman 的疏忽，因为该公司未能就枪手与 ChatGPT 的令人不安的对话向当局发出警报。佛罗里达州成为本月早些时候起诉 OpenAI 的美国第一个州，指责该公司通过向学校枪击者提供信息、提供自残指导和让年轻用户上瘾来伤害儿童。该州总检察长还针对 OpenAI 涉嫌参与枪击事件展开刑事调查。路透社贡献了报道

articles.title: Can smartphones help explain the drop in birth rates?
articles.summary: Are smartphones causing people to have fewer children?  A provocative new working paper explores the persistent drop in birth rates since the iPhone was introduced nearly two decades ago.
articles.titleZh: 智能手机可以帮助解释出生率下降吗？
articles.summaryZh: 智能手机是否导致人们少生孩子？  一份颇具争议性的新工作论文探讨了自近二十年前推出 iPhone 以来出生率持续下降的问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Apple CEO Steve Jobs unveils the iPhone in 2007. A new working paper suggests the spread of smartphones helps explain the persistent decline in birth rates in the nearly two decades since.
contentParagraphs.zh: 苹果公司首席执行官史蒂夫·乔布斯 (Steve Jobs) 于 2007 年推出了 iPhone。一份新的工作论文表明，智能手机的普及有助于解释此后近二十年出生率持续下降的原因。

contentParagraphs.en: David Paul Morris/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 大卫·保罗·莫里斯/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: David Paul Morris/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 大卫·保罗·莫里斯/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Sign up for the Planet Money newsletter. The world is confusing. Economics can help.
contentParagraphs.zh: 订阅 Planet Money 时事通讯。世界很混乱。经济学可以提供帮助。

contentParagraphs.en: Economist Caitlin Myers has a striking explanation for why women are having fewer babies: It's the smartphones.
contentParagraphs.zh: 对于女性生育数量减少的原因，经济学家凯特琳·迈尔斯给出了一个惊人的解释：原因在于智能手机。

contentParagraphs.en: Myers and other researchers have been searching for what's behind the sharp drop in fertility over the last two decades. Birth rates in the U.S. have fallen by 22% since 2007.
contentParagraphs.zh: 迈尔斯和其他研究人员一直在寻找过去二十年生育率急剧下降背后的原因。自 2007 年以来，美国的出生率下降了 22%。

contentParagraphs.en: At first, economists assumed that the Great Recession was to blame but that births would soon rebound, as they'd done after previous downturns.
contentParagraphs.zh: 起初，经济学家认为大衰退是罪魁祸首，但出生率很快就会反弹，就像之前的经济衰退后所做的那样。

contentParagraphs.en: But then the economy recovered — and birth rates just kept falling.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但随后经济复苏，出生率持续下降。

contentParagraphs.en: If the recession wasn't responsible for the baby bust, what was?
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果经济衰退不是造成婴儿潮的原因，那么是什么造成的呢？

contentParagraphs.en: "Whatever it is, it must be big, and it needs to coincide with about 2007 because that's when we see all the births go down," says Myers, a professor of economics at Middlebury College in Vermont.
contentParagraphs.zh: 佛蒙特州米德尔伯里学院 (Middlebury College) 经济学教授迈尔斯 (Myers) 表示：“无论是什么，它都一定很大，而且需要与 2007 年左右一致，因为那时我们会看到所有出生人数下降。”

contentParagraphs.en: That happens to be the year that Apple CEO Steve Jobs introduced the iPhone, declaring, "Every once in a while, a revolutionary product comes along that changes everything."
contentParagraphs.zh: 那一年恰好是苹果公司首席执行官史蒂夫·乔布斯推出 iPhone 的一年，他宣称：“每隔一段时间，就会有一款革命性产品出现，改变一切。”

contentParagraphs.en: Maybe even birth rates.
contentParagraphs.zh: 甚至可能是出生率。

contentParagraphs.en: In a provocative new working paper titled "Is the iPhone Birth Control?" Myers argues that the spread of smartphones could explain between a third and a half of the decline in birth rates during that period.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在一份具有挑衅性的新工作论文中，题为“iPhone 是节育吗？”迈尔斯认为，智能手机的普及可以解释这一时期出生率下降的三分之一到一半。

contentParagraphs.en: Births fell more in places where you could get an iPhone in the early years
contentParagraphs.zh: 在早年可以买到 iPhone 的地方，出生率下降得更多

contentParagraphs.en: To test that theory, she makes clever use of an accident of history that creates a kind of natural experiment. When iPhones first came out, they worked only with AT&T.
contentParagraphs.zh: 为了检验这一理论，她巧妙地利用了一次历史事故，创造了一种自然实验。 iPhone 刚推出时，只与 AT&T 合作。

contentParagraphs.en: "In some areas of the country, AT&T had broadband coverage and you could get an iPhone, and in other areas, including where I live in Vermont, that coverage was much more limited," Myers recalls. "And what you can see in this simplest of comparisons, births start to fall in the places where you can get one, and they're not falling nearly as much in the places where you can't."
contentParagraphs.zh: “在美国的某些地区，AT&T 有宽带覆盖，你可以买一部 iPhone，而在其他地区，包括我住在佛蒙特州的地方，覆盖范围要有限得多，”迈尔斯回忆道。 “从这个最简单的比较中你可以看到，在可以生育的地方，出生率开始下降，而在不能生育的地方，出生率的下降幅度就没有那么大了。”

contentParagraphs.en: One might argue the results are skewed because smartphones spread faster in urban areas or wealthier communities. But the results hold up even when Myers controlled for variables like population density and local economics.
contentParagraphs.zh: 有人可能会说，这一结果存在偏差，因为智能手机在城市地区或较富裕社区的普及速度更快。但即使迈尔斯控制了人口密度和当地经济等变量，结果仍然成立。

contentParagraphs.en: "You're probably not going to get pregnant if you're not interacting with people in person"
contentParagraphs.zh: “如果你不与人面对面交流，你可能不会怀孕”

contentParagraphs.en: The drop in birth rates has affected women of all ages, but it's most pronounced among teenagers. That sounds plausible to Jean Twenge, a professor of psychology at San Diego State University.
contentParagraphs.zh: 出生率下降影响了所有年龄段的女性，但在青少年中最为明显。对于圣地亚哥州立大学心理学教授让·特温格来说，这听起来似乎有道理。

contentParagraphs.en: In books like Generations: The Real Differences Between Gen Z, Millennials, Gen X, Boomers, and Silents, Twenge has documented the profound behavioral changes that accompanied smartphones, especially among young people.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在《世代：Z世代、千禧一代、X世代、婴儿潮一代和沉默一代之间的真正差异》等书中，特温格记录了智能手机带来的深刻的行为变化，尤其是在年轻人中。

contentParagraphs.en: "The smartphone fundamentally changed the way adolescents spent their time outside of school," Twenge told NPR. "They started spending a lot more time online and on their phones and a lot less time hanging out with their friends in person and driving around in a car or going to the mall or just hanging out."
contentParagraphs.zh: “智能手机从根本上改变了青少年在校外度过时间的方式，”特温格告诉 NPR。 “他们开始花更多的时间在网上和手机上，而减少了与朋友见面、开车、去购物中心或只是闲逛的时间。”

contentParagraphs.en: Myers says it's not a stretch to think that this would result in fewer babies.
contentParagraphs.zh: 迈尔斯表示，毫不夸张地说，这会导致婴儿数量减少。

contentParagraphs.en: "If there's one thing I learned in abstinence-only sex ed in the '90s in Georgia growing up, it's that you're probably not going to get pregnant if you're not interacting with people in person — if you're not having sex," Myers says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “如果说我在 20 世纪 90 年代在佐治亚州的禁欲性教育中学到了一件事的话，那就是如果你不与人面对面交流，如果你不发生性​​行为，你可能就不会怀孕，”迈尔斯说。

contentParagraphs.en: In the paper, co-authored with her 24-year-old stepson, Ezekiel Hooper, Myers suggests smartphones also placed access to information about contraceptives and abortion in the palm of users' hands.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在这篇与她 24 岁的继子 Ezekiel Hooper 共同撰写的论文中，迈尔斯表示，智能手机还可以让用户掌握有关避孕和堕胎的信息。

contentParagraphs.en: The devices also might have depressed birth rates by making it easier for people to find pornography.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些设备还可能让人们更容易找到色情内容，从而降低出生率。

contentParagraphs.en: "When I talk to my students at Middlebury College, this is the first one they actually bring up," Myers says. "Pornography was proving to be a substitute for in-person relationships."
contentParagraphs.zh: “当我与米德尔伯里学院的学生交谈时，这是他们真正提到的第一个问题，”迈尔斯说。 “事实证明，色情可以替代面对面的关系。”

contentParagraphs.en: Apple didn't respond to an inquiry about Myers' paper.
contentParagraphs.zh: 苹果没有回应有关迈尔斯论文的询问。

contentParagraphs.en: Eventually, copycat phones came along that could be used on other networks, and today smartphones are ubiquitous. Myers says that this raises the question of whether birth rates will level off now or continue to fall.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最终，可以在其他网络上使用的山寨手机出现了，如今智能手机已经无处不在。迈尔斯表示，这提出了一个问题：出生率现在会趋于平稳还是继续下降。

contentParagraphs.en: "I think it's possible that we'll continue to see effects of phones on behavior and outcomes like fertility for years to come," she says. "But we'll just have to keep watching."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我认为，未来几年我们可能会继续看到手机对行为和结果（如生育能力）的影响，”她说。 “但我们只能继续观察。”

contentParagraphs.en: Apple is a financial supporter of NPR.
contentParagraphs.zh: Apple 是 NPR 的财务支持者。

articles.title: Socioeconomic factors are becoming 'biologically embedded' in children's brains
articles.summary: A study of more than 2,300 9- to 10-year-olds found that socioeconomic factors explained most differences in the preteens' brain development.
articles.titleZh: 社会经济因素正在“生物学地嵌入”儿童的大脑中
articles.summaryZh: 一项针对 2,300 多名 9 至 10 岁儿童的研究发现，社会经济因素可以解释青少年大脑发育的大部分差异。

contentParagraphs.en: A new study finds that the socioeconomics of a preteen's neighborhood can leave a distinctive pattern in their brain.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一项新的研究发现，青春期前的邻居的社会经济学可以在他们的大脑中留下独特的模式。

contentParagraphs.en: The most powerful factors affecting a child's brain development involve socioeconomic opportunities, according to a study in the journal Science.
contentParagraphs.zh: 《科学》杂志上的一项研究表明，影响儿童大脑发育的最强大因素涉及社会经济机会。

contentParagraphs.en: The analysis of more than 2,300 9- and 10-year-olds found that environmental factors ranging from household income to education to neighborhood quality are associated with brain differences that can clearly be seen in MRI scans.
contentParagraphs.zh: 对 2,300 多名 9 岁和 10 岁儿童的分析发现，从家庭收入到教育再到社区质量等环境因素都与大脑差异有关，这些差异可以在 MRI 扫描中清楚地看到。

contentParagraphs.en: The researchers also found that preteens who'd grown up in neighborhoods with lower incomes and limited social support had brain differences associated with less sleep and more stress.
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究人员还发现，在收入较低和社会支持有限的社区长大的青春期前儿童的大脑差异与睡眠较少和压力较大有关。

contentParagraphs.en: "Something is going on in these neighborhoods," says Scott Marek, the study's first author and an assistant professor of radiology at WashU School of Medicine. "We need to find out how socioeconomics is becoming biologically embedded."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这些社区正在发生一些事情，”该研究的第一作者、华盛顿大学医学院放射学助理教授斯科特·马雷克说。 “我们需要找出社会经济学如何融入生物学。”

contentParagraphs.en: The research "highlights the fact that the environment in which we grow up and live has powerful impacts on our brain," says Russell Poldrack, a psychology professor at Stanford University who was not involved in the study.
contentParagraphs.zh: 斯坦福大学心理学教授拉塞尔·波尔德拉克（Russell Poldrack）没有参与这项研究，他说，这项研究“强调了一个事实，即我们成长和生活的环境对我们的大脑有着巨大的影响”。

contentParagraphs.en: It also challenges earlier research that focused on links between brain development and factors like IQ and mental health.
contentParagraphs.zh: 它还对早期关注大脑发育与智商和心理健康等因素之间联系的研究提出了挑战。

contentParagraphs.en: Those factors do appear to have a small influence on brain development, says Dr. Nico Dosenbach, an author of the new study and a professor at WashU Medicine in St. Louis.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这项新研究的作者之一、圣路易斯华盛顿大学医学院的教授尼科·多森巴赫博士说，这些因素似乎确实对大脑发育有很小的影响。

contentParagraphs.en: "But socioeconomics was, by a wide margin, absolutely the dominant variable," Dosenbach says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “但社会经济学在很大程度上绝对是主导变量，”多森巴赫说。

contentParagraphs.en: As a result, some earlier studies linking cognitive performance to brain differences "may require reevaluation," says Dr. Theodore D. Satterthwaite, an associate professor at the University of Pennsylvania Perelman School of Medicine.
contentParagraphs.zh: 因此，一些早期将认知表现与大脑差异联系起来的研究“可能需要重新评估”，宾夕法尼亚大学佩雷​​尔曼医学院副教授西奥多·D·萨特思韦特博士说。

contentParagraphs.en: Those studies focused on factors like IQ or mental health without accounting for socioeconomics, says Satterthwaite, who co-authored a perspective piece that accompanied the new study. So a reevaluation including that variable could weaken or even negate the findings.
contentParagraphs.zh: 萨特思韦特说，这些研究关注的是智商或心理健康等因素，没有考虑社会经济学。萨特思韦特是这项新研究附带的一篇观点文章的共同作者。因此，包括该变量在内的重新评估可能会削弱甚至否定研究结果。

contentParagraphs.en: In fact, the new study adds to what Satterthwaite calls a "rising tide of research" over the past few years, suggesting that childhood environment has a powerful influence on brain development.
contentParagraphs.zh: 事实上，这项新研究补充了萨特思韦特所说的过去几年的“研究浪潮”，表明童年环境对大脑发育有强大的影响。

contentParagraphs.en: Lots of brains, lots of variables
contentParagraphs.zh: 很多大脑，很多变数

contentParagraphs.en: The goal of the new research was to take an unbiased look at brain development and consider every factor that might have an influence.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这项新研究的目标是公正地看待大脑发育，并考虑可能产生影响的每一个因素。

contentParagraphs.en: Data came from the federally funded Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) Study, which is tracking thousands of children starting at ages 9 and 10.
contentParagraphs.zh: 数据来自联邦政府资助的青少年大脑认知发展 (ABCD) 研究，该研究正在跟踪数千名 9 至 10 岁的儿童。

contentParagraphs.en: The researchers used brain scans from ABCD to identify differences in the organ's structure and communication networks. Then they looked to see whether those differences were associated with factors like a child's environment, cognitive abilities and mental health.
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究人员使用 ABCD 的脑部扫描来识别器官结构和通讯网络的差异。然后他们研究这些差异是否与儿童的环境、认知能力和心理健康等因素有关。

contentParagraphs.en: Finally, the team ranked each factor by how strongly it was associated with brain differences.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最后，研究小组根据每个因素与大脑差异的关联程度对每个因素进行了排名。

contentParagraphs.en: "The pattern that emerged was, at first, very confusing to us," Marek says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “起初，出现的模式让我们非常困惑，”马雷克说。

contentParagraphs.en: Nearly all of the top-ranked factors were in some way related to socioeconomic opportunity. And these factors were associated primarily with brain differences in areas involved in sensory processing and motor control, not higher functions like attention or memory.
contentParagraphs.zh: 几乎所有排名靠前的因素都在某种程度上与社会经济机会有关。这些因素主要与大脑中涉及感觉处理和运动控制的区域的差异有关，而不是与注意力或记忆等高级功能有关。

contentParagraphs.en: So the team worked to figure out how factors like income, preschool enrollment, healthcare access and neighborhood quality might be affecting brain development.
contentParagraphs.zh: 因此，该团队致力于找出收入、学前教育入学率、医疗保健服务和社区质量等因素如何影响大脑发育。

contentParagraphs.en: The apparent answer involves brain circuits involved in keeping someone awake and alert. These circuits are altered when children get less sleep, experience more stress, or spend a lot of time using social media.
contentParagraphs.zh: 显而易见的答案涉及到使人保持清醒和警觉的大脑回路。当孩子睡眠减少、压力增大或花大量时间使用社交媒体时，这些回路就会发生改变。

contentParagraphs.en: The team found that all of those environmental factors are more prevalent in neighborhoods where children lack economic, educational and social opportunities.
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究小组发现，所有这些环境因素在儿童缺乏经济、教育和社会机会的社区中更为普遍。

contentParagraphs.en: The finding doesn't prove that these factors are actually causing the brain differences, Marek says, "But the data are screaming that we should be looking at sleep, stress and screens if we want to get somewhere."
contentParagraphs.zh: 马雷克说，这一发现并不能证明这些因素实际上导致了大脑差异，“但数据告诉我们，如果我们想有所进步，就应该关注睡眠、压力和屏幕。”

articles.title: Iraq’s paramilitary groups say they will disarm. Will that ever happen?
articles.summary: Powerful Shia leader Muqtada al-Sadr among those who say their groups will integrate into the state.
articles.titleZh: 伊拉克准军事组织表示他们将解除武装。这种事会发生吗？
articles.summaryZh: 强大的什叶派领导人穆克塔达·萨德尔表示，他们的团体将融入国家。

contentParagraphs.en: It was his first speech in front of parliament as Iraq’s prime minister, and Ali al-Zaidi was quick to set out his stall.“[I am committing to] reforming the security apparatus by restricting weapons to state control and strengthening the capabilities of the security forces,” al-Zaidi pledged in mid-May.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3One in 70 people worldwide is forcibly displaced: UNHCRlist 2 of 3Which World Cup players and officials have faced visa troubles in the US?list 3 of 3How Lebanon became the breaking point for the Iran war ceasefireend of listAl-Zaidi is not the first Iraqi prime minister to promise that the state will have a monopoly on arms in a country where paramilitary groups – including many backed by neighbouring Iran – have been powerful since the 2003 United States-led war on Iraq.But with pressure from Washington to disarm the groups amid the US-Israel war on Iran, and the economic challenges brought on by that war, al-Zaidi knows that he needs to clamp down on the power of Iraq’s paramilitary groups to attract outside investment, and not attract the ire of the US.A number of the groups have played a role during the regional conflict, launching missiles and drones at US facilities in Iraq and the Gulf.Iraqi oil revenue has sharply declined since the beginning of the war in the region in late February and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s main conduits for oil. Iraq had exported about 3.3 million barrels per day (bpd) before the war, roughly 90 percent of it through the Strait of Hormuz. Figures for March show oil exports dropping to about 600,000bpd. Oil revenue represents more than 90 percent of Iraq’s state budget.“Neither the economy not stability can flourish while arms remain out of the state’s control,” political analyst Mujashaa Altimimi told Al Jazeera. “Addressing this issue has become more of an economic necessity than it is a security one for the prime minister.”Al-Sadr leads the wayOne of the most powerful Iraqi Shia leaders, Muqtada al-Sadr, was quick to back al-Zaidi when he announced on May 27 that the Saraya al-Salam group would separate from the political movement under al-Sadr’s control and integrate its members into the state’s armed forces.“Complying with the national interest of the state and to avoid the dangers threatening our homeland, it is our obligation to announce the complete dissociation of Saraya al-Salam from the National Shia Movement in order to fully integrate them into the state under the military general commander,” al-Sadr said in a statement welcomed by al-Zaidi.Al-Sadr also called on other paramilitary groups – in particular, those affiliated with the Iran-backed and largely Shia Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF) – to follow his lead and dissolve themselves.Some have promised to do so, including Asaib Ahl al-Haq, a US-designated Foreign Terrorist Organization.Faleh al-Fayyad, the head of the PMF, also said there would be a “complete disengagement” between the PMF and any political groups, adding that the goal was to make the PMF “an institution subject to a unified system and linked to the commander-in-chief of the armed forces [the Iraqi prime minister]”.But other groups, including the powerful Kataib Hezbollah and Harakat al-Nujaba, have rejected compliance with the government’s call to restrict weapons to the state.Path forward unclearSpeaking to Al Jazeera, one of Iraq’s most influential Shia figures, who did not wish to be named, said the groups that rejected the government’s efforts would be sidelined, but added the integration process would have to be done carefully.“[A lengthy] process to dismantle these factions is better than confrontation, which may cause bloodshed,” said the figure, who requested anonymity. “Those who reject the government’s call will find themselves alone. And will find out that they were mistaken.”But even with the groups that have agreed to comply with the state’s order, there are questions over implementation and what comes next.Will powerful groups that have built up military might over years agree to simply hand over their weapons? If so, what will they expect in return?Al-Sadr, for example, previously “retired” from politics in 2022, after his supporters attempted to overrun parliament during a political crisis. While he has since maintained a boycott of parliamentary elections, he did officially rebrand his political movement from the Sadrist Movement to the National Shia Movement, indicating that he is not fully done with politics yet.A popular figure among large numbers of Iraqi Shia, al-Sadr may be positioning himself for a future where powerful political parties are more important than armed groups.“I think he wants to send a message that ‘although I am not part of the political scene, I can still influence it,'” said Rahman al-Jebouri, the head of the Political Leadership and Governance Development Academy. “I believe this is a smart gesture.”Al-Jebouri believes that al-Sadr has shown he has a “clear understanding” of the push in the wider region, with US pressure, for disarming non-state actors – and this may also be another indication that he is thinking of returning to politics.“It is difficult to predict how he thinks,” said al-Jebouri. “But I believe he now has solid ground for a possible return to the political scene.”The next practical step in the dissolution of Iraq’s paramilitary groups is yet unclear. Analysts believe the process will be long, complicated, and full of unforeseen developments.One of the solutions currently under discussion is the formation of a new security ministry that would incorporate various security forces, including the PMF and the Kurdish Peshmerga.“It is too early to be optimistic,” Iraqi political analyst Hani Ashour told Al Jazeera. “It is better to think about the reality with limited optimism. Let’s wait and see how things will turn out.”
contentParagraphs.zh: 这是他作为伊拉克总理首次在议会面前发表演讲，阿里·扎伊迪很快就摆出了自己的立场。“[我承诺]通过将武器限制在国家控制之下来改革安全机构，并加强安全部队的能力，”扎伊迪在五月中旬许诺。 推荐故事列表 3 项列表 1 全世界 70 人中就有一个被迫流离失所：联合国难民署列出 3 项中的 2 哪些世界杯球员和官员有在美国遇到签证麻烦？清单 3（共 3） 黎巴嫩如何成为伊朗战争停火的突破点 扎伊迪并不是第一个承诺国家将在这个国家拥有武器垄断权的伊拉克总理，自 2003 年美国领导的伊拉克战争以来，准军事组织 — — 其中包括许多得到邻国伊朗支持的 — — 一直很强大。受那场战争的影响，扎伊迪知道他需要压制伊拉克准军事组织的力量，以吸引外部投资，而不是引起美国的愤怒。许多组织在地区冲突中发挥了作用，向美国在伊拉克和海湾的设施发射导弹和无人机。自2月底该地区战争爆发以及世界主要石油通道之一霍尔木兹海峡关闭以来，伊拉克石油收入急剧下降。战前伊拉克每天出口约 330 万桶 (bpd)，其中约 90% 通过霍尔木兹海峡出口。 3 月份的数据显示石油出口量降至约 60 万桶/日。石油收入占伊拉克国家预算的 90% 以上。“如果武器仍不受国家控制，经济和稳定都无法蓬勃发展，”政治分析家穆贾沙阿·阿尔蒂米米 (Mujashaa Altimimi) 告诉半岛电视台。 “对于总理而言，解决这一问题已成为一种经济需要，而不是安全问题。” 萨德尔带头 伊拉克最有权势的什叶派领导人之一穆克塔达·萨德尔 (Muqtada al-Sadr) 很快就支持了扎伊迪，他于 5 月 27 日宣布萨拉亚·萨拉姆组织将脱离萨德尔控制下的政治运动，并将其成员纳入国家武装部队。萨德尔在一份受到扎伊迪欢迎的声明中表示：“为了保护国家并避免威胁我们祖国的危险，我们有义务宣布萨拉亚·萨拉姆完全脱离国家什叶派运动，以便将他们完全融入到军事总司令领导下的国家。”有些组织已承诺这样做，其中包括被美国认定为外国恐怖组织的阿赛卜·阿尔·哈克。PMF 负责人法勒·法耶兹也表示，PMF 与任何政治团体之间将“完全脱离接触”，并补充说，目标是使 PMF“成为一个受统一制度管辖并与武装部队总司令（伊拉克总理）挂钩的机构”。强大的真主党旅和努贾巴党拒绝遵守政府关于限制国家武器的呼吁。前进的道路不明朗，一位不愿透露姓名的伊拉克最有影响力的什叶派人物之一在接受半岛电视台采访时表示，拒绝政府努力的团体将被边缘化，但他补充说，整合进程必须谨慎进行。流血，”这位要求匿名的人士说道。 “那些拒绝政府号召的人将会发现自己孤立无援，并且会发现自己错了。”但是，即使那些同意遵守国家命令的团体，在执行和接下来会发生什么方面也存在问题。多年来积累了军事实力的强大团体会同意简单地交出武器吗？如果是这样，他们会期望得到什么回报？例如，萨德尔在政治危机期间试图超越议会后，于 2022 年“退出”政坛。虽然此后他一直抵制议会选举，但他确实正式将自己的政治运动从萨德尔运动更名为全国什叶派运动，这表明他还没有完全结束政治生涯。萨德尔是众多伊拉克什叶派中受欢迎的人物，他可能正在为一个强大的政党比武装团体更重要的未来做好准备。“我认为他想传达一个信息，即‘虽然我不属于政治舞台，但我仍然可以影响它，’”拉赫曼说。 al-Jebouri，政治领导和治理发展学院院长。 “我认为这是一个明智的举动。”杰布里相信，萨德尔已经表明，他对在美国压力下推动更广泛地区解除非国家行为体武装的行动有着“清晰的认识”——这也可能是他正在考虑重返政坛的另一个迹象。“很难预测他的想法，”杰布里说。 “但我相信他现在有可能重返政治舞台的坚实基础。” 解散伊拉克准军事组织的下一步实际步骤尚不清楚。分析人士认为，这一过程将是漫长、复杂的，并且充满了不可预见的事态发展。目前正在讨论的解决方案之一是组建一个新的安全部，该部将纳入包括PMF和库尔德自由斗士在内的各种安全部队。“现在乐观还为时过早，”伊拉克政治分析家哈尼·阿舒尔告诉半岛电视台。 “最好以有限的乐观态度来思考现实。让我们拭目以待，看看事情会如何发展。”

articles.title: The Guardian view on the analogue resurgence: the shock of the old | Editorial
articles.summary:
Long-abandoned formats such as cassettes and VHS tapes are finding new life as consumers seek a digital detox
Ten years after the last video recorder manufacturer ceased production, the first straight-to-video movie for two decades – This Is How the World Ends – was released this
articles.titleZh: 《卫报》对模拟复兴的看法：旧的冲击 |社论
articles.summaryZh:
随着消费者寻求数字戒毒，盒式磁带和 VHS 磁带等长期废弃的格式正在寻找新的生命
最后一家录像机制造商停产十年后，二十年来第一部直接视频电影《世界是这样结束的》于今年上映

contentParagraphs.en: Ten years after the last video recorder manufacturer ceased production, the first straight-to-video movie for two decades – This Is How the World Ends – was released this month. The resurgence of vinyl began long ago; sales are at their highest level for over 30 years. But record buyers enthuse about the warmth of their sound and the generous visual expanse of album covers. In contrast, the new movie is shot in HD; the director acknowledges that those watching it on video will see a cropped, fuzzier image. The point of the exercise – beyond creating a buzz – lies not in the inherent qualities of VHS, but the effect of its rarity on the viewer.When everything is available in high definition with one swipe of your screen, cumbersome physical formats that must be hunted down appear both nostalgically inviting and strikingly fresh. Last year, Taylor Swift’s The Life of a Showgirl was released in multiple physical formats, including cassette and CD – technically digital, but also enjoying a revival thanks to its retro feel. The title track of her previous album, The Tortured Poets Department, mocked a lover’s attachment to his typewriter, notoriously favoured by hipsters.Older adults may yearn for their past (as in last year’s BBC drama Mix Tape). Younger consumers are seizing on formats that are new to them and the novel allure of an offline world. These media are often “presented as the remedy for our digital ailment” – in contrast to new technologies depicted as “addictive, unnatural, unhealthy and harmful” – writes the communications lecturer ARE Taylor in The Analogue Idyll, a new book that he has edited. This year’s vogue for the “analogue bag”, containing items such as physical books and craft materials, is all about cutting screen time. Weekends away are marketed as digital detoxes. In the US, parenting influencers have boasted of turning back to VHS, not only to avoid inappropriate algorithmic recommendations, but also to teach patience.Robert dos Santos. Photograph: Migal Van AsIn the age of AI, the yearning for the tangible, the imperfect and the human is especially potent. Unease at big tech’s exploitation of consumers is evident not only in the desire to own rather than lease songs, movies and books, but also the broader revolt against relentless optimisation and convenience. Vinyl “was more fun precisely because it was less efficient”, says David Sax in his 2016 book The Revenge of Analog. Listeners must cherish the easily damaged format and can’t skip or rearrange tracks without hovering over the record player. The music commands attention instead of fading into the background.Yet businesses have found fresh opportunities in the backlash to the new and shiny. In 2023, Kodak released its first Super 8 camera for over three decades – with an LCD display and SD card. Apps make iPhone photos resemble old Polaroids. And This Is How the World Ends will ultimately be streamed.The remark by the film’s director, Robert dos Santos, that for now “you need to be part of the club to watch this” will suggest to some a refreshing willingness to make demands on the audience and embrace the weird – rejecting the beigification of culture. Others will hear the old desire to distinguish oneself with rarefied consumption. There is plenty to enjoy in the analogue. But as Dr Taylor’s new book reminds us, old-fashioned formats are no more “authentic” than the digital ones – and individual consumer choices to retreat from the online world won’t fix its problems.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最后一家录像机制造商停产十年后，二十年来第一部直接视频电影《世界是这样结束的》于本月上映。黑胶唱片的复兴很久以前就开始了。销售额达到 30 多年来的最高水平。但唱片购买者对其温暖的声音和专辑封面的宽阔视觉效果充满热情。相比之下，新电影是高清拍摄的；导演承认，那些在视频中观看的人会看到一个被裁剪的、更模糊的图像。除了引起轰动之外，这项练习的目的不在于 VHS 的固有品质，而在于其稀有性对观众的影响。当只需轻轻一划屏幕即可以高清方式观看所有内容时，必须寻找的繁琐的物理格式就显得既怀旧又诱人，而且非常新鲜。去年，泰勒·斯威夫特 (Taylor Swift) 的《歌舞女郎的一生》以多种物理格式发行，包括卡带和 CD——技术上是数字化的，但由于其复古的感觉，也受到了复兴。她上一张专辑的主打歌《The Tortured Poets Department》嘲笑了恋人对打字机的依恋，这在潮人中广为人知。老年人可能会怀念自己的过去（就像去年 BBC 电视剧《Mix Tape》中那样）。年轻消费者正在抓住对他们来说新鲜的形式以及线下世界的新奇吸引力。传播学讲师 ARE Taylor 在他编辑的新书《模拟田园诗》中写道，这些媒体经常被“描述为治疗我们数字疾病的良方”——与被描述为“令人上瘾、不自然、不健康和有害”的新技术形成鲜明对比。今年流行的“模拟包”，里面装着实体书籍和工艺材料等物品，目的就是为了减少屏幕时间。周末休假被宣传为数字排毒。在美国，有影响力的育儿人士吹嘘要回归 VHS，不仅是为了避免不适当的算法建议，也是为了培养耐心。罗伯特·多斯桑托斯 (Robert dos Santos)。摄影：Migal Van As 在人工智能时代，人们对​​有形的、不完美的和人类的渴望尤其强烈。人们对大型科技公司对消费者的剥削感到不安，不仅表现在人们渴望拥有而不是租赁歌曲、电影和书籍，而且还表现在对不断优化和便利的更广泛的反抗。 David Sax 在其 2016 年出版的《模拟的复仇》一书中表示，黑胶唱片“更有趣，恰恰是因为它效率较低”。听众必须珍惜这种容易损坏的格式，如果不将鼠标悬停在电唱机上，就无法跳过或重新排列曲目。音乐吸引了人们的注意力，而不是淡出背景。然而，企业在对新鲜事物的强烈抵制中发现了新的机会。 2023 年，柯达发布了三十多年来的首款 Super 8 相机，配备 LCD 显示屏和 SD 卡。应用程序使 iPhone 照片看起来像旧的宝丽来照片。 《这就是世界的终结》最终将在流媒体上播出。电影导演罗伯特·多斯桑托斯表示，目前“你需要成为俱乐部的一员才能观看这部电影”，这将向一些人暗示一种令人耳目一新的意愿，即向观众提出要求并拥抱怪异——拒绝文化的北化。其他人会听到旧有的愿望，即通过稀有的消费来脱颖而出。模拟中有很多值得享受的地方。但正如泰勒博士的新书提醒我们的那样，老式格式并不比数字格式更“真实”——个人消费者选择退出网络世界并不能解决问题。

articles.title: What is El Niño and why could it mean record temperatures?
articles.summary: Global temperatures are expected to rise in the coming months as El Niño begins.

contentParagraphs.en: What is El Niño and why could it mean record temperatures?22 hours agoMark Poynting and Esme StallardBBC News Climate & ScienceGetty ImagesIndonesia was hit by extreme fires during the 2015-16 El Niño A natural weather pattern called El Niño - which could bring extreme weather to many parts of the world - has begun, US scientists say.The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) said that El Niño conditions are likely to strengthen over the rest of 2026. Many forecasts suggest it could be one of the strongest ever El Niños.Coming on top of decades of human-caused warming, 2027 may be the hottest year on record, with disruption to weather, food supplies and economies.What happens during an El Niño and why could this one be strong?An El Niño develops in the Pacific Ocean and the atmosphere above it.When the winds that typically blow east-to-west weaken or reverse, warmer water can spread across the central and eastern tropical Pacific.Scientists at NOAA announced that a new El Niño phase had started after observing sea surface temperatures more than 0.5C above average in the central tropical Pacific. They also noticed a switch in atmospheric conditions, with a drop in pressure over the central Pacific compared with the western Pacific.The Japanese Meteorological Agency also said that El Niño conditions are present.Some scientists have warned that this El Niño could be especially strong, partly because the water beneath the surface of the Pacific is unusually warm.These waters have been about 6C above average in places, according to the UN's World Meteorological Organization. Deep-sea heat is often followed by warmer waters at the surface. A "very strong" or so-called "super" El Niño is when the warming of the central tropical Pacific Ocean surface reaches 2C or more over an extended period. These events have only happened a few times since 1950. The NOAA said there was a 63% chance of this El Niño ending up "very strong". That would "rank among the largest El Niño events in the historical record going back to 1950", it added.It is expected to last at least into early 2027. The El Niño phenomenon was first observed by Peruvian fishermen in the 1600s, who nicknamed it El Niño de Navidad - Christ Child in Spanish.How could a strong El Niño affect the weather?A strong El Niño event would "exacerbate drought and heavy rainfall and increase the risk of heatwaves both on land and in the ocean", said World Meteorological Organization secretary general Celeste Saulo.During El Niño, the ocean will transfer heat to the air, making it warmer.Coupled with higher global temperatures from human-caused climate change, this could make 2027 the hottest year on record for the planet. The exact impact on the weather depends on where you are and what time of year it is. No two El Niños are the same, but a strong event typically fuels hot, dry weather in parts of South America, South East Asia and Australia, raising the chances of droughts and wildfires.It can also weaken the Indian monsoon. In the southern US, heavier rainfall can increase the risks of flooding.El Niño tends to bring more tropical storms in the eastern and central Pacific, but fewer in the tropical Atlantic, including the south-east US.The way UK weather is affected is complicated, and can vary. But El Niño may increase the chance of a mild start and cold end to UK winter, according to the Met Office.What impact could El Niño have on people?The UN's secretary general, António Guterres, has warned the world to prepare. "El Niño conditions will pour fuel on the fire of a warming world. Impacts will hit even harder, travel even farther, and cross borders with devastating speed," he said.Droughts in parts of South America and South East Asia could hit crops at a time when the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is already disrupting the distribution of fertiliser. This could mean smaller harvests, reduced food supply and higher prices.For fishing communities in South America, there is the risk of smaller catches. During El Niño, less cold, nutrient rich water comes to the surface, reducing food availability for marine species such as anchovies.Some scientists are drawing comparisons with the 2015-16 El Niño, one of the strongest ever recorded.At that time there were water shortages in the Caribbean, a record-breaking storm season in the central Pacific, and drought in the Horn of Africa.The combination of storm events and widespread drought - at least partly the result of El Niño - led to food shortages affecting millions of people around the world, according to the UN's Food and Agricultural Organization.Getty ImagesFishing stocks can be affected by the effects of El NiñoIs climate change affecting El Niño?El Niño events since 1950 have been stronger than those between 1850 and 1950, according to the UN's climate scientists, the IPCC.But it said that tree rings and other historical evidence show there have been variations in their frequency and strength since the 1400s.The IPCC said there is no clear evidence that climate change has affected El Niño events.Some climate models suggest that El Niño episodes could become more frequent and more intense as a result of global warming - although this is a complex and uncertain area of science with no clear consensus. But the impacts of El Niño will occur on top of those from long-term climate change - which could fuel increasingly severe weather extremes. What is La Niña?El Niño has a sister weather pattern called La Niña.During these events, the temperature of the surface of the sea in the central-eastern Pacific is cooler - the opposite of what is seen during El Niño. Atmospheric pressure is also higher than usual in the central Pacific, and lower than usual in the west. La Niña typically brings wetter conditions to parts of Australia, Indonesia and equatorial South America, and drier conditions to the southern US.El Niño and La Niña often alternate, but sometimes we can have two of
contentParagraphs.zh: 什么是厄尔尼诺现象？为什么它会意味着创纪录的气温？22小时前马克·坡印廷和埃斯梅·斯塔拉德BBC新闻气候与科学盖蒂图片社在2015-16厄尔尼诺期间印度尼西亚遭受极端火灾袭击美国科学家称，一种称为厄尔尼诺的自然天气模式已经开始，它可能会给世界许多地区带来极端天气。美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）表示，厄尔尼诺现象可能会在2015-16年加强2026 年剩下的时间里。许多预测表明，这可能是有史以来最强烈的厄尔尼诺现象之一。除了几十年来人为造成的变暖之外，2027 年可能是有记录以来最热的一年，天气、粮食供应和经济都会受到干扰。厄尔尼诺现象期间会发生什么？为什么这次会很强烈？厄尔尼诺现象在太平洋及其上方的大气中发展。当通常从东向西吹的风减弱或逆转时，温暖的海水可能会扩散到整个太平洋热带太平洋中部和东部。美国国家海洋和大气管理局(NOAA)的科学家在观测到热带太平洋中部海面温度比平均水平高出0.5摄氏度以上后宣布，新的厄尔尼诺现象已经开始。他们还注意到大气条件的变化，与西太平洋相比，中太平洋的压力下降。日本气象厅还表示，厄尔尼诺现象已经存在。一些科学家警告说，这次厄尔尼诺现象可能特别强烈，部分原因是太平洋表面以下的水异常温暖。根据联合国世界气象组织的数据，这些水域的温度比平均水平高出约6摄氏度。深海高温过后，表层海水通常会变暖。 “非常强”或所谓的“超级”厄尔尼诺现象是指热带太平洋中部海面升温在较长时间内达到2摄氏度或以上。自 1950 年以来，此类事件只发生过几次。NOAA 表示，这次厄尔尼诺现象有 63% 的可能性最终“非常强烈”。报告补充说，这将“跻身自 1950 年以来历史记录中最大的厄尔尼诺事件之一”。预计至少会持续到 2027 年初。厄尔尼诺现象是秘鲁渔民在 1600 年代首次观察到的，他们将其昵称为 El Niño de Navidad，西班牙语中的基督圣婴。强烈的厄尔尼诺现象如何影响天气？强烈的厄尔尼诺现象将“加剧干旱和强降雨，世界气象组织秘书长塞莱斯特·索洛表示，“增加陆地和海洋热浪的风险”。厄尔尼诺现象期间，海洋会将热量传递到空气中，导致空气变暖。再加上人为造成的气候变化导致全球气温升高，2027年可能成为地球有记录以来最热的一年。对天气的确切影响取决于您所在的位置以及一年中的什么时间。没有两次厄尔尼诺现象是相同的，但强烈的厄尔尼诺现象通常会加剧南美洲、东南亚和澳大利亚部分地区的炎热干燥天气，增加干旱和野火的可能性。它还会削弱印度季风。在美国南部，降雨量增加会增加洪水风险。厄尔尼诺现象往往会在东太平洋和中太平洋带来更多热带风暴，但在热带大西洋（包括美国东南部）带来的热带风暴较少。英国天气受到影响的方式很复杂，而且各不相同。但据英国气象局称，厄尔尼诺现象可能会增加英国冬季温和开始和寒冷结束的可能性。厄尔尼诺现象会对人们产生什么影响？联合国秘书长安东尼奥·古特雷斯已警告世界做好准备。他说：“厄尔尼诺现象将为全球变暖火上浇油。其影响将更加严重、传播更远，并以毁灭性的速度跨越边界。”在霍尔木兹海峡的有效关闭已经扰乱化肥分配之际，南美和东南亚部分地区的干旱可能会袭击农作物。这可能意味着收成减少、粮食供应减少和价格上涨。对于南美洲的渔业社区来说，存在渔获量减少的风险。厄尔尼诺现象期间，到达地表的寒冷、营养丰富的水流减少，减少了凤尾鱼等海洋物种的食物供应。一些科学家将其与 2015-16 年厄尔尼诺现象进行比较，这是有记录以来最强烈的厄尔尼诺现象之一。当时加勒比地区出现缺水，太平洋中部出现破纪录的风暴季节，非洲之角出现干旱。风暴事件和大范围干旱（至少部分是厄尔尼诺现象的结果）相结合，导致粮食短缺，影响了数百万人。根据联合国粮食及农业组织的数据，渔业资源可能会受到厄尔尼诺现象的影响气候变化是否会影响厄尔尼诺现象？根据联合国气候科学家IPCC的说法，1950年以来的厄尔尼诺事件比1850年至1950年间的厄尔尼诺事件更强。但它表示，树木年轮和其他历史证据表明，自1400年代以来，厄尔尼诺事件的频率和强度一直存在变化。没有明确的证据表明气候变化影响了厄尔尼诺事件。一些气候模型表明，由于全球变暖，厄尔尼诺现象可能会变得更加频繁和强烈——尽管这是一个复杂且不确定的科学领域，目前还没有明确的共识。但厄尔尼诺现象的影响将发生在长期气候变化的影响之上，这可能会加剧日益严重的极端天气。什么是拉尼娜现象？厄尔尼诺现象有一个姊妹天气模式，称为拉尼娜现象。在这些事件期间，中东部太平洋的海面温度较低，与厄尔尼诺现象期间的情况相反。太平洋中部的大气压力也高于平常，而西部地区则低于平常。拉尼娜现象通常会给澳大利亚、印度尼西亚和南美洲赤道地区的部分地区带来湿润的天气，而给美国南部带来干燥的天气。厄尔尼诺和拉尼娜经常交替出现，但有时我们可以同时出现两种现象

articles.title: Musk’s xAI fired engineer for raising concerns about Grok chatbot, lawsuit claims
articles.summary:
Former xAI engineer Devin Kim alleges he was illegally fired for trying to implement safety mechanisms for the chatbot
A former engineer at Elon Musk’s xAI who now heads a thinktank focused on AI safety filed a lawsuit claiming he was fired from the SpaceX subsidiary for raising
articles.titleZh: 诉讼称，马斯克的 xAI 解雇了对 Grok 聊天机器人提出担忧的工程师
articles.summaryZh:
前 xAI 工程师 Devin Kim 声称他因试图为聊天机器人实施安全机制而被非法解雇
埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) xAI 的一名前工程师现在领导一家专注于人工智能安全的智囊团，他提起诉讼，声称自己因筹集资金而被 SpaceX 子公司解雇。

contentParagraphs.en: A former engineer at Elon Musk’s xAI who now heads a thinktank focused on AI safety filed a lawsuit claiming he was fired from the SpaceX subsidiary for raising concerns about the risks artificial intelligence poses to humanity.Devin Kim claims in the lawsuit filed in California state court on Tuesday that his efforts to place guardrails on the development of the chatbot Grok made him a target for company leadership.The lawsuit comes ahead of SpaceX’s planned initial public offering, the largest ever, on Friday.“Mr Kim repeatedly complained that xAI’s failure to prioritize AI safety, particularly with respect to Grok, virtually guaranteed that the Company would commit unlawful acts, from fomenting discrimination to proliferating weapons of mass destruction,” the lawsuit says.xAI and SpaceX did not immediately respond to requests for comment on Kim’s lawsuit.The day Kim’s lawsuit was filed, an official Canadian watchdog said in a report that xAI’s Grok chatbot had violated the country’s privacy laws by launching an image-generation tool that allowed users to create and share non-consensual, sexualized deep fakes.After the privacy commissioner, Philippe Dufresne, launched a formal investigation in January, xAI announced tweaks to prevent Grok from allowing the editing of images of real people in revealing clothing. Britain and Canada are two in a growing list of countries cracking down on explicit content generated by Grok.The non-profit Center for AI Safety, which focuses on the risks potentially posed by AI, announced last week that it had named Kim as its president.Musk, the world’s richest person, established xAI in 2023 as what he said would be a safer alternative to OpenAI, which he had helped found more than a decade ago. A jury last month rejected Musk’s lawsuit claiming that OpenAI had strayed from its original mission to benefit humanity.According to the new lawsuit, Kim was one of the initial hires at xAI in 2024 and was promoted to a key leadership position months after joining the company.Kim said Musk expected xAI to implement appropriate safety testing and processes. But Kim’s supervisor, the xAI co-founder Jimmy Ba, flouted those directives and rejected Kim’s insistence on implementing safety mechanisms, the lawsuit claims.Kim says Ba abruptly fired him last September just before Kim was set to give a presentation on AI safety to company leadership.The lawsuit accuses xAI and SpaceX of retaliation and wrongful discharge in violation of California law, and seeks unspecified monetary damages.SpaceX and Musk’s other ventures have been dogged by alleged safety issues, including hazards posed to company employees, concerns about self-driving technology and lawsuits over its chatbot’s output.xAI has faced multiple lawsuits and international investigations over its Grok AI product in recent months, after a period when the chatbot generated millions of AI-altered sexualized images earlier this year. Many of these sexualized images were created using photos of women without their consent, according to researchers at the Center for Countering Digital Hate, which also estimated that Grok produced about 23,000 sexualized images of children over an 11-day period in December and January.Musk has denied any knowledge of Grok producing child sexual abuse material, stating in January that he was “not aware of any naked underage images generated by Grok. Literally zero.” The company added restrictions to Grok’s image generation capabilities in earlier January following backlash and threats of regulatory action from multiple countries.
contentParagraphs.zh: 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的 xAI 前工程师、现在专注于人工智能安全的智囊团负责人提起诉讼，声称他因提出对人工智能对人类构成的风险的担忧而被 SpaceX 子公司解雇。德文·金 (Devin Kim) 在周二向加利福尼亚州法院提起的诉讼中声称，他为聊天机器人 Grok 的开发设置障碍的努力使他成为公司领导层的目标。该诉讼发生在 SpaceX 计划于周五进行有史以来规模最大的首次公开募股之前。诉讼称，“xAI 未能优先考虑人工智能安全，特别是在 Grok 方面，这实际上保证了该公司会犯下非法行为，从煽动歧视到扩散大规模杀伤性武器。” xAI 和 SpaceX 没有立即回应对 Kim 诉讼的置评请求。 Kim 提起诉讼的当天，加拿大官方监管机构在一份报告中表示，xAI 的 Grok 聊天机器人推出了一款允许用户创建和分享的图像生成工具，违反了该国的隐私法。在隐私专员 Philippe Dufresne 一月份发起正式调查后，xAI 宣布进行调整，以防止 Grok 允许编辑真实人物暴露服装的图像。英国和加拿大是越来越多打击 Grok 生成的露骨内容的国家中的两个。专注于人工智能潜在风险的非营利性人工智能安全中心上周宣布任命 Kim 为主席。世界首富马斯克于 2023 年创立了 xAI，他表示 xAI 将是他十多年前帮助创建的 OpenAI 的更安全替代品。上个月，陪审团驳回了马斯克的诉讼，指控 OpenAI 偏离了造福人类的初衷。根据新诉讼，Kim 是 xAI 2024 年的首批员工之一，并在加入公司几个月后晋升为关键领导职位。Kim 表示，马斯克希望 xAI 实施适当的安全测试和流程。但诉讼称，Kim 的主管、xAI 联合创始人吉米·巴 (Jimmy Ba) 无视这些指令，并拒绝了 Kim 坚持实施安全机制的要求。Kim 表示，去年 9 月，就在 Kim 准备向公司领导层发表有关人工智能安全的演讲之前，Ba 突然解雇了他。该诉讼指控 xAI 和 SpaceX 违反加州法律进行报复和错误排放，并要求赔偿金额不详。SpaceX 和马斯克的其他企业一直受到所谓安全问题的困扰，包括对公司员工构成的危险、对自动驾驶技术的担忧以及对其聊天机器人输出的诉讼。近几个月来，xAI 因其 Grok AI 产品面临多起诉讼和国际调查，今年早些时候，该聊天机器人生成了数百万张经过人工智能修改的性感图像。据反数字仇恨中心的研究人员称，其中许多色情图像是在未经女性同意的情况下使用女性照片创建的。该中心还估计，Grok 在 12 月和 1 月的 11 天内制作了约 23,000 张儿童色情图像。马斯克否认了解 Grok 制作儿童性虐待材料，并在 1 月份表示，他“不知道 Grok 生成了任何裸体未成年人图像。几乎为零。”在遭到多个国家的强烈反对和监管行动威胁后，该公司于 1 月初对 Grok 的图像生成功能进行了限制。

articles.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
articles.summary: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.
articles.titleZh: 科学家称厄尔尼诺现象正在发生并威胁极端天气
articles.summaryZh: 美国科学家表示，厄尔尼诺现象已经正式开始，引发了人们对极端天气和高温的担忧。

contentParagraphs.en: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say22 hours agoMatt McGrath,Environment correspondent,Simon King,Lead Weather PresenterandMark Poynting,Climate reporterEPA/ShutterstockEl Niño - the natural Pacific weather pattern that pushes up global temperatures - has officially begun, US scientists say.The US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) has declared that El Niño conditions are now under way in the tropical Pacific, with sea surface temperatures having risen sharply in recent months.Many forecasts suggest this could end up as a so-called "super" El Niño, and even be among the strongest ever recorded.Coming on top of decades of human-caused warming, it could bring another record-hot year - most likely in 2027 - with disruption to weather, food supplies and economies running well into that year.This announcement by NOAA is not a surprise as forecasters have expected this warming phase, after the cooler "sister" pattern, La Niña, ended earlier this year.Sea surface temperatures in the central and tropical Pacific have now passed the 0.5C-above-average threshold that US scientists use to define an El Niño event."El Niño conditions developed over the past month, as shown by above-average sea surface temperatures (SSTs) across the central to eastern equatorial Pacific Ocean," the agency said.NOAA has also seen the winds above the equatorial Pacific begin to shift - a sign that the atmosphere is now responding to the warmer ocean, not just the ocean warming on its own.What has surprised the researchers is how confident the computer models already are about its strength.El Niño's intensity is measured by how far sea surface temperatures rise above average in a key zone of the Pacific.A strong event is defined as more than 1.5C above average; a very strong one above 2C.According to NOAA's June outlook, "there is a 63% chance of a very strong El Niño during November-January, that would rank among the largest El Niño events in the historical record going back to 1950," the agency said.The three strongest events since then have been in 1982/83, 1997/98 and 2015/16.Some of the latest US and European (ECMWF) models go further, showing temperatures in the tropical Pacific potentially climbing more than 3C above average by the end of the year. But the US agency urged some caution on what their strength prediction implies."Even very strong El Niño events do not lead to the expected impact everywhere, but stronger events can more significantly tilt the odds in favour of expected outcomes."The bigger concern is that all this is happening on an already much hotter planet."We do need to worry about the impacts," said Prof Adam Scaife, head of monthly to decadal prediction at the UK Met Office."The current El Niño is… riding on top of a substantial amount of global warming."This means that the actual temperatures in affected regions could well be unprecedented, as the warming from El Niño is being topped up by climate change."A very strong El Niño typically lifts global air temperatures by around 0.2C, releasing heat stored in the ocean into the atmosphere. That extra blast now lands on a world that is already setting records.The year 2024 - the warmest on record - was boosted by an El Niño that was not even especially strong.And despite the cooling drag of a La Niña event, 2025 still came in as the third warmest year on record, hotter even than the super El Niño year of 2016."At the end of this year and into 2027, we're likely to see very high temperatures globally," Prof Scaife said."In 2027, we're likely to see excess heat on top of the global warming we've already got, and that could easily lead to another year above 1.5 degrees [of warming above late-19th-Century levels]."EPA-EFE/REX/ShutterstockA farmer in Zambia shows a small ear of corn grown in a field impacted by drought during a previous El Niño event.No two El Niños are alike, but the disruption is felt most sharply in the tropics.Flooding is common in northern Peru and southern Ecuador, and can reach parts of East Africa, Central Asia and the southern United States.At the same time, the risk of drought and wildfire rises across much of Australia, Indonesia and northern South America - hitting agriculture and global food stocks.El Niño also tends to suppress Atlantic hurricanes, and forecasters already expect a quieter-than-average season."While that sounds like a good thing, for Central America that leads to a lot less rainfall and potentially drought conditions," said Liz Stephens, professor of climate risk and resilience at the University of Reading.Even the UK feels it, if faintly: El Niño can tilt the odds towards a mild start and cold end to winter, though the links are loose.For many, the forecast is far from abstract."An El Niño declaration is not just another weather forecast - for millions of people it is a deadly siren to be feared," said Mohamed Adow, director of campaign group Power Shift Africa."It means failed rains, dying crops, rising food prices, and families pushed to the edge yet again. In East Africa especially, this will land on communities already battered by droughts and floods in recent years."Japan's Meteorological Agency (JMA) takes a similar view to NOAA, judging that El Niño conditions are present. It adds it is all but certain to last into the autumn.Not every agency is ready to call it, though. Australia's Bureau of Meteorology (BoM) uses a stricter criterion, requiring sea surface temperatures to exceed 0.8C above average.This week it said the tropical Pacific was "approaching El Niño conditions", with central Pacific temperatures already crossing its thresholds, but it stopped short of formally declaring the event had begun.It expects El Niño to develop later this year, and says it could be strong.El Niño occurs every two to seven years and usually lasts about a year.There is still no conclusive proof that climate change is making these events stronger or more frequent - bu
contentParagraphs.zh: 科学家表示，厄尔尼诺现象正在发生，并威胁极端天气 22小时前，环境记者马特·麦格拉思、首席天气主持人西蒙·金和气候记者马克·坡印廷 美国科学家表示，厄尔尼诺现象——推高全球气温的太平洋自然天气模式——已经正式开始。美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）宣布，热带太平洋目前正在出现厄尔尼诺现象，海面温度近几个月来急剧上升。许多预测表明，这可能最终成为所谓的“超级”厄尔尼诺现象，甚至是有记录以来最强的厄尔尼诺现象之一。在数十年人为造成的变暖之后，它可能会带来另一个创纪录的炎热年份 - 最有可能在 2027 年 - 天气、粮食供应和经济都会受到干扰。 NOAA 的这一宣布并不令人意外，因为在较凉爽的“姐妹”模式拉尼娜现象于今年早些时候结束之后，NOAA 的这一宣布并不令人意外。今年。中部和热带太平洋的海面温度现已超过美国科学家用来定义厄尔尼诺事件的平均水平0.5摄氏度的阈值。该机构表示：“厄尔尼诺现象在过去一个月中发展，赤道太平洋中东部至东部海面温度（SST）高于平均水平就表明了这一点。”美国国家海洋和大气管理局还发现，赤道太平洋上空的风向开始发生转变，这表明大气正在对海洋变暖做出反应，而不仅仅是海洋变暖。令研究人员惊讶的是，计算机模型对其强度充满信心。厄尔尼诺现象的强度是通过太平洋关键区域海面温度高于平均水平的程度来衡量的。强事件的定义是比平均水平高出 1.5 摄氏度以上；高于 2C 的非常强的厄尔尼诺现象。根据 NOAA 6 月份的展望，“11 月至 1 月期间有 63% 的机会出现非常强的厄尔尼诺现象，这将成为自 1950 年以来历史记录中最大的厄尔尼诺事件之一。”该机构表示。此后最强的三个事件发生在 1982/83 年、1997/98 年和 2015/16 年。美国和欧洲的一些最新事件(ECMWF) 模型更进一步，显示到今年年底，热带太平洋的气温可能会比平均水平上升 3 摄氏度以上。但美国机构敦促人们对其强度预测的含义保持谨慎。“即使非常强烈的厄尔尼诺事件也不会在所有地方产生预期的影响，但更强的事件可以更显着地倾斜预期结果的可能性。”“更令人担忧的是，这一切都发生在一个已经更热的星球上。“我们确实需要担心影响，”英国气象局月度到十年预测负责人亚当·斯凯夫教授说。“目前的厄尔尼诺现象...... “这意味着受影响地区的实际气温很可能是史无前例的，因为厄尔尼诺现象导致的变暖正在因气候变化而加剧。”非常强烈的厄尔尼诺现象通常会使全球气温升高约0.2摄氏度，将海洋中储存的热量释放到大气中，这股额外的热量现在已经创下了记录。2024年是有记录以来最温暖的一年，但厄尔尼诺现象的强度并不是特别强。尽管拉尼娜现象发生，2025年仍然是有记录以来第三个最热的年份，甚至比2016年的超级厄尔尼诺年还要热。斯凯夫教授说：“从今年年底到2027年，我们可能会在全球范围内看到非常高的气温。”“2027年，我们可能会在已经出现的全球变暖的基础上看到多余的热量，这很容易导致另一年气温超过1.5度[变暖程度高于 19 世纪末水平]。“EPA-EFE/REX/Shutterstock 赞比亚的一位农民展示了在上一次厄尔尼诺事件期间受到干旱影响的田地里种植的小玉米穗。没有两次厄尔尼诺现象是相似的，但在热带地区感受到的破坏最为严重。洪水在秘鲁北部和厄瓜多尔南部很常见，并且可以到达东非、中亚和美国南部的部分地区。同时，澳大利亚、印度尼西亚和南美洲北部大部分地区发生干旱和野火的风险上升，对农业和全球粮食库存造成打击。厄尔尼诺现象也往往会抑制大西洋飓风，预报员已经预计季节会比平均水平安静。雷丁大学气候风险与复原力教授利兹·斯蒂芬斯表示，“虽然这听起来是一件好事，但对中美洲来说，这会导致降雨量大大减少，并可能导致干旱。”就连英国也隐隐约约地感觉到了：厄尔尼诺现象可能会倾斜“厄尔尼诺现象不仅仅是另一个天气预报，对数百万人来说，这是一个令人恐惧的致命警报，”非洲权力转移运动组织主任穆罕默德·阿多说，“这意味着降雨不足、庄稼枯死、粮食价格上涨，家庭再次被推向边缘。”特别是在东非，这将降临到近年来已经遭受干旱和洪水袭击的社区。“日本气象厅（JMA）与 NOAA 持类似观点，判断厄尔尼诺现象已经存在。它补充说，厄尔尼诺现象几乎肯定会持续到秋季。不过，并不是每个机构都准备好迎接这种情况。澳大利亚气象局（BoM）采用了更严格的标准，要求海面温度比平均水平高出 0.8 摄氏度。本周它表示热带太平洋正在“接近厄尔尼诺现象”，太平洋中部的气温已经超过其阈值，但它没有正式宣布该事件已经开始。预计厄尔尼诺现象将在今年晚些时候发展，并表示可能会很强烈。厄尔尼诺现象每两到七年发生一次，通常持续一年左右。目前还没有确凿的证据表明气候变化正在使这些事件变得更强或更频繁 -
